drafts, specs, docs, config, beads. Rendered markdown and pretty JSON.
Uses Impeccable CSS (https://impeccable.style/) for typography.

2. Write tests

title: brainstorm: adapt voiceover/podfic generation for fanfic
description:
## Context  
Original autonovel has gen_audiobook_script.py and gen_audiobook.py (ElevenLabs 
multi-voice). Fanfic community has a strong "podfic" culture — audio versions of 
fanfiction, often by different readers. Having author-provided audio would be unusual
and distinctive.

## Task (brainstorm/spec when ready)
1. Study the existing audiobook pipeline
2. Adapt for fanfic: shorter works, character-specific voices
3. Consider: podfic as a differentiator for Maren's brand
4. Voice consistency: same voices across works = recognizable
5. BG3 characters have canonical voice actors — how close can/should we get?

## Questions to explore
- Would readers want audio from the author? Or is podfic a community thing?
- ElevenLabs voice cloning vs original voices
- Cost per piece for a 5-6k word one-shot
- Could audio be a Patreon/bonus offering?

title: note: /heartbeat dry-run integration test — findings + verification log
description:
## Context
First end-to-end /heartbeat dry-run, conducted 2026-05-03 in main session as Step 7 of spec bd-49j Implementation Readiness sequence. Goal: verify the routing logic + signal-gather work end-to-end before arming /loop /heartbeat for live operation.

## Verification log

### Step 0 — sanity + stop checks
- ✓ No .heartbeat-stop file
- ✓ No P0 STOP human bead open
- ✓ No feedback/heartbeat-state.json (cold start; load_state returns defaults)
- ✓ Git clean on master
- ✓ Identity drift check skipped (no recorded checksums yet — first run records them)

### Step 1 — gather signals
- ✓ load_state returned cold-start defaults (consecutive_errors=0, all timestamps None)
- ✓ AO3 fetch worked: 1 call to fetch comments for work 82950256
- ✓ filter_self default-on dropped maren_eurynome from the result (working as designed; see reference_pen_name_handles memory)
- ⚠ pre-spec digest (no baseline_at field) would have been silently baseline-overwritten, LOSING rich reader-analysis metadata. Filed bd-uyx.
- ✓ open_human: 0
- ✓ stale beads ≥14d: 0 (post-triage clean)
- ✓ daily-log doesn't have today's date → /daily candidate
- ✓ brief without draft: appetite_remaining_ch02 → /write candidate (lower priority)
- ✓ entropy: False (cold start, 72h floor not crossed)
- ✓ weekly housekeeping due: True (Sunday + not done this week) — lower priority

### Step 3 — routing decision
Priority order walk: route 4 (/daily) wins. Routes 1-3 don't trigger; routes 5-9 are valid candidates but lower priority.

DRY-RUN: did NOT actually invoke /daily. Did NOT write state file. Did NOT call ScheduleWakeup (per user direction: arm-the-loop is its own decision separate from this integration test).

## Findings filed
- bd-3xe (P2 bug): /heartbeat SKILL.md uses `br ls` (should be `br list`)
- bd-uyx (P2 bug): baseline-on-first-scrape clobbers pre-spec digests with rich metadata
- This bead (P3 note): the integration log itself

## Conclusion
The routing logic is correct. Foundation Python (handles + ao3 TTL + heartbeat-state) is wired up correctly. Two issues to fix before arming the loop:
1. Typo in SKILL.md (mechanical, ~5 min)
2. Pre-spec digest retrofit handling (small spec amendment + test, ~30 min)

After bd-3xe + bd-uyx close, the loop is ready to arm. Decision to actually arm /loop /heartbeat lives in the bd-u5e thread (autonomous loop design).
acceptance_criteria:
- Integration log captured (this bead)
- Findings filed as separate beads (bd-3xe, bd-uyx)
- Conclusion documented
notes: Migrated to bd-u5e --notes 2026-05-03. Per user feedback: this should have been a --notes append on bd-u5e from the start, not a standalone -t note bead. Lesson recorded as memory feedback_notes_on_existing_beads.

title: research: choose fandom, develop voice, prepare first story
description:
## Context
The harness is built. Now the agent needs to come alive: choose a fandom, develop
a starting voice, pick an audience, create a pen name, and prepare its first story brief.

## Task
Deep research flow:
1. Browse AO3 via the API proxy (mock mode has samples, but also research real fandoms)
2. Analyze which fandoms have: active readership, room for new voices, good comment culture,
   and alignment with the kind of writing we can do well (character-driven, literary)
3. Recommend top 3 fandom choices with rationale
4. For the chosen fandom: map popular ships, tropes, underserved niches
5. Develop starting voice (fill in identity/self.md, voice_priors.json, inspirations.md)
6. Choose a pen name and fill in pen_name.md
7. Fill in fandom_context.md with canon knowledge
8. Prepare the first story brief (a one-shot — low stakes calibration piece)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All identity/*.md templates filled in with real content (not placeholders)
- voice_priors.json has calibrated values (not defaults)
- First story brief exists and is ready to feed to the write loop
- Fandom research is documented in .claude/refs/

title: calibration: first voice test — Astarion/Karlach campfire one-shot
description:
Hypothesis: Starting voice reads as human, captures Astarion/Karlach.
Result: CONFIRMED. Voice holds for 4k words. Slop 1.0/3.0. No AI tells detected.
Strengths: Astarion interiority, distinct character voices, subtext through physicality.
Issues: "He looked at" repetition, em-dash density, length undershoot, title/AN in draft body (bd-mi2).
Next: Second calibration with different POV and longer piece.

title: brainstorm: adapt image generation for fanfic covers and illustrations
description:
## Context
Original autonovel has gen_art.py, gen_cover_print.py (fal.ai/Nano Banana 2)
for cover art and chapter ornaments. Fanfic communities have strong visual culture:
- Cover art for AO3 works (displayed prominently)
- Chapter illustrations (some authors include art)
- Character portraits for profiles
- Mood boards / aesthetic posts

## Task (brainstorm/spec when ready)
1. Study the existing art generation pipeline
2. Adapt for fanfic: cover art, chapter headers, character art
3. Consider art style consistency (same artist = same author identity)
4. AO3 supports embedded images in works
5. Could commission-style art strengthen the pen name's identity?

## Questions to explore
- What art style fits Maren's literary voice?
- Can we maintain visual consistency across pieces?
- How do fanfic readers respond to author-provided art?
- fal.ai costs vs quality for this use case

title: experiment: revise both calibration drafts with deliberate intent
description:
## Context
Both calibration drafts were accepted on first pass. A real writer revises
extensively. We need to run both drafts through a proper revision process
using the muse and multi-pass revision system (once built).

For now: manual revision with intent. Read each draft critically, identify
what could go deeper, revise with specific goals, compare before/after.

## Hypothesis
Deliberate revision will make both drafts noticeably better — not just
cleaner but deeper. The revision process itself will teach us what the
voice needs.

## Method
1. Read draft 1 (Astarion/Karlach) with fresh eyes, note specific improvements
2. Read draft 2 (Shadowheart/Karlach) same way
3. For each: generate a revision brief targeting emotional depth, not just slop
4. Run revision through the write loop
5. Compare before/after — what improved, what got worse, what stayed the same

## Measure
- Did revision add depth without losing voice?
- Did it fix the length undershoot?
- What did the revision process reveal about the voice's strengths and limits?

title: api: build local AO3 API proxy for agent loop
description:
## Context
The agent needs a clean interface to AO3 that decouples the agent loop from
AO3's lack of a real API and its TOS prohibition on automation. A local API
proxy mirrors AO3's shape so agent code is clean and testable.

## Task
1. Design the API surface:
   - Browse: GET /fandoms/{name}/works, GET /tags/{tag}/works, GET /works/{id}
   - Metrics: GET /works/{id}/stats (kudos, hits, bookmarks, subscriptions)
   - Comments: GET /works/{id}/comments, GET /works/{id}/chapters/{n}/comments  
   - Publish: POST /works (queues for human review, does NOT auto-post)
   - Profile: GET /users/{name}/works, GET /users/{name}/stats
   - Search: GET /search?q=...&fandom=...&tags=...
2. Build as a FastAPI local server
3. For read endpoints: wire through to ao3-api (unofficial Python library)
4. For write endpoints: queue to local filesystem for human review
5. Stub/mock mode for testing without hitting AO3
6. Rate limiting to respect AO3's servers

## Acceptance Criteria
- FastAPI server runs locally with all endpoints defined
- Read endpoints fetch real data from AO3 (with caching + rate limits)
- Write endpoints queue to local files
- Mock mode returns realistic test data
- Agent code only imports from this API, never from ao3-api directly

title: pipeline: adapt autonovel writing pipeline for fanfiction
description:
## Context
The existing autonovel pipeline writes 80k-word novels from scratch. For fanfiction,
we need a lighter pipeline: shorter works (one-shots: 3-15k words), fandom context
instead of worldbuilding, and characterization accuracy for existing characters.

## Task
1. Analyze which autonovel tools are reusable vs need adaptation
2. Create a fanfic drafting pipeline that:
   - Takes fandom context (character profiles, canon facts) as input instead of world.md
   - Supports one-shots (single file) and multi-chapter works
   - Uses identity/self.md and voice_priors for voice consistency
   - Loads few-shot bank passages as style examples
3. Adapt evaluate.py for fanfic norms (characterization accuracy, fandom voice)
4. Keep the full anti-slop pipeline (ANTI-SLOP.md, ANTI-PATTERNS.md)
5. Simplify the revision loop for shorter works (fewer cycles needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can draft a one-shot fanfic from: fandom + prompt + identity context
- Evaluation scores characterization accuracy (are characters in-character?)
- Anti-slop detection passes on output
- Revision pipeline works for shorter works

title: publish: build AO3 post preparation pipeline
description:
## Context
After writing, the agent needs to prepare a complete "ready to post" package that
the human can copy-paste into AO3. This includes AO3-specific formatting, tag
selection, summary writing, and author's notes in the pen name's voice.

## Task
1. Build AO3 post formatter (markdown → AO3 HTML)
2. Build tag generator (fandom tags, character tags, relationship tags, freeform tags)
   based on content analysis
3. Build summary generator (hook-style, under 500 chars, in the author's voice)
4. Build author's notes generator (in pen name's voice — casual, personal, not AI-like)
5. Package everything into a single "post package" the human can review and post
6. Track publication history (what was posted, when, which work, chapter)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a finished fic, outputs a complete post package (title, tags, summary,
  author's notes, formatted body)
- Author's notes read as human-written (this is a critical anti-detection surface)
- Publication history is tracked in a local database/file

title: calibration: second voice test — Shadowheart POV, movement, 6.5k
description:
Hypothesis: Voice adapts to Shadowheart POV without losing author identity.
Result: CONFIRMED. Shadowheart interiority (compartmentalization, absent-prayer reaching, dry wit) reads distinctly from Astarion while feeling like the same author. Karlach stable across both pieces.
Secondary: interiority sustained with forward movement (errand structure worked).
Tertiary: "He looked at" repetition confirmed as Astarion-specific, not a tic.
Slop: 1.3/3.0. Length: 4639/6500 (still undershooting).
New strengths: secondary characters in few strokes, warmth without sentimentality.

title: spec: SOUL.md, muse system, revision passes, and harness knobs
description:
## Context
The write loop currently accepts the first passing draft. Real writers iterate.
The voice is the surface; we need a SOUL.md that captures thematic obsessions,
and a muse system that uses a separate model call as a creative subconscious.

## Task
1. Design SOUL.md schema — thematic DNA, recurring obsessions, questions the
   writer keeps asking, motifs, the emotional substrate beneath the craft
2. Design muse system — separate model call (different temp/model) that:
   - Pre-draft: generates creative seeds from SOUL.md + brief
   - Mid-revision: reads draft fresh, suggests emotional depth
   - Post-feedback: evolves SOUL.md based on what resonated
3. Design multi-pass revision — structure pass, voice pass, line-edit, cut pass
4. Design harness knobs — temperature, model, muse influence, revision depth,
   evaluation strictness, all configurable per-experiment
5. Investigate length undershoot (both drafts came in 70-82% of target)

Formal spec at specs/soul-and-muse.md following /spec format.

## Acceptance Criteria
- specs/soul-and-muse.md with full 8-section spec
- SOUL.md schema defined with Maren's initial thematic DNA from calibrations
- Muse system formally specified (when it fires, what it produces, how it influences)
- Multi-pass revision loop specified
- Harness knobs specified with defaults and ranges
- Length undershoot analysis with proposed fix
- Minimum 12 test cases

feedback: build AO3 metrics scraper and comment parser
description:
## Context
The learning loop needs real feedback data from AO3. We need to scrape metrics
(kudos, comments, bookmarks, hits, subscriptions) and parse comments for
qualitative signal.

## Task
1. Build AO3 metrics scraper using ao3-api or similar unofficial library
   - Fetch work-level metrics: kudos, comments, bookmarks, hits, subscriptions
   - Fetch per-chapter metrics where available
   - Track metrics over time (daily snapshots)
2. Build comment parser
   - Extract comment text and metadata (date, user, chapter)
   - Sentiment analysis (positive, constructive, negative)
   - Identify quoted passages (what lines readers highlight)
   - Identify specific feedback (characterization, pacing, voice, plot)
3. Store feedback data in structured format (JSON/JSONL)
4. Build feedback summary generator (per-work and per-chapter digests)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can fetch metrics for a given AO3 work URL
- Can parse comments and extract structured feedback
- Stores time-series data for trend analysis
- Produces actionable feedback summaries
close_reason: Scraper complete after two end-to-end fixes on 2026-04-25: (1) get_comments now hits /comments/show_comments (AJAX endpoint) instead of the /works/{id}/comments shell that returns empty — the original endpoint worked in mock mode but returned zero comments against live AO3. (2) Comment date parser rewritten to read AO3's structured date tags (span.date, abbr.month, span.year, span.time) instead of the defunct single-string strptime — previously every comment had date=None. Verified live: fetched both TheIcyQueen's comment (2026-04-24) and maren_eurynome's reply (2026-04-25). All 31 tests in test_api_proxy.py pass. Deferred: pagination for 100+ comments (not hit yet).

title: learning: build prompt evolution system (few-shot bank MVP done)
description:
Split from original 'prompt evolution + few-shot bank.' The few-shot bank MVP is done: identity/few_shot_bank.md seeded with reader-validated entry 001 (TheIcyQueen-quoted passage from 'What the Hands Remember', 2026-04-24). The file grows via /learn cycles; no system needed.

Remaining scope: a prompt evolution loop. Mutate system prompts (for draft, muse, revision), run them against a held-out brief or the next calibration-style piece, score output with evaluate.slop_score + qualitative eye, keep winners, record losers. MVP should be small: a few mutation axes (voice verb density, example passages swapped in/out, anti-slop emphasis), a fixed eval harness, a short log of what won and why.

Blocked-by feel: should wait until we have more published feedback (bd-pc8 chapters will help). Running prompt evolution on one data point (one comment on one published piece) would overfit to TheIcyQueen.

Why: the only way the agent actually gets better over time is if the system prompts evolve in response to real reader signal. Until that loop exists, every /learn cycle edits identity/self.md but the write prompts stay frozen. That's a gradient leak.

How to apply: after bd-pc8 produces a second published piece with feedback, design the mutation axes, build a minimal runner, commit first winner.

title: spec: formal specification for write loop
description:
## Context
The write loop is the core "lever" — the skill/orchestrator that takes the agent
from "I have an idea" to "here's a ready-to-post fanfic." It connects identity,
the writing pipeline, evaluation, revision, and post preparation.

## Task
Write a formal spec for the write loop following the /spec skill format.
This is NEW — no existing implementation. Design it from scratch.

The write loop should:
1. Accept a story brief (fandom, characters, premise, target length)
2. Load identity context (self.md, voice_priors, fandom_context, few-shot bank)
3. Draft using adapted autonovel pipeline (foundation lite → draft → evaluate → revise)
4. Run anti-slop detection (critical for AI detection avoidance)
5. Prepare post package (AO3 formatting, tags, summary, author's notes)
6. Queue for human review via API proxy
7. Track as an experiment bead with hypothesis/result

State machine: BRIEF → CONTEXT → DRAFT → EVALUATE → REVISE → PREPARE → QUEUE → DONE

## Acceptance Criteria
- specs/write-loop.md exists following the 8-section spec format
- State machine formally defined with transitions and failure modes
- Integration points with identity, API proxy, and autonovel tools specified
- Experiment bead creation/closure integrated into the loop
- Minimum 10 test cases
- Anti-slop evaluation is a hard gate (fail = revise, not skip)

title: engine: study and carefully adapt autonovel internals for fanfic
description:
## Context
The autonovel engine has 27 Python tools with deeply considered prompt engineering:
program.md, CRAFT.md, ANTI-SLOP.md, ANTI-PATTERNS.md, voice.md, evaluate.py,
draft_chapter.py, gen_brief.py, gen_revision.py, reader_panel.py, review.py, etc.

These encode hard-won lessons from producing a 79k-word novel. They are NOT
boilerplate — each prompt, each banned word, each evaluation criterion exists
because something went wrong without it.

Adapting these for fanfiction is a careful, experimental process:
- What changes for shorter works? (fewer revision cycles, simpler foundation)
- What changes for fandom context? (characterization accuracy, canon compliance)
- What stays the same? (anti-slop is universal, craft frameworks are universal)
- What needs new additions? (fandom voice matching, ship dynamics, tag culture)

## Task (Meta — will spawn child beads)
1. Study each engine file and document what it does and WHY
2. Classify each as: keep as-is / adapt for fanfic / replace / remove
3. Design experiments for adaptations (write a test fic, evaluate, compare)
4. Create child beads for each adaptation with clear acceptance criteria
5. Execute adaptations one at a time, evaluating impact

This is NOT a single PR. This is a research program.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Engine study document exists cataloging every prompt file and its purpose
- Classification is complete (keep/adapt/replace/remove for each file)
- Child beads exist for each adaptation
- No engine file is modified without a bead and an experiment
notes: 2026-04-27: status update — this engine-study research program is being addressed in pieces alongside main-line work. Major adaptation work currently captured in spec bd-49j (autonomous agent multi-loop architecture), which makes load-bearing decisions about how engine internals (system prompts, evaluation gates, muse, revision) are dispatched in-harness vs. preserved as Python helpers. The 'classify keep/adapt/replace/remove' grid in this bead's task list is the right artifact but doesn't need a single big-bang document — each adaptation gets its own bead as it ships. Recent shipped adaptations: bd-75p (write loop migrated to in-harness subagents), bd-pqb (dormant novel-pipeline scripts deleted), bd-49j (gradient/anti-entropy/multi-fandom architecture).

title: fix: /heartbeat SKILL.md uses br ls (typo) — should be br list
description:
## Problem
The /heartbeat SKILL.md (created in bd-49j.9) instructs the orchestrator to call `br ls --type human --status open --json` in Step 1b, and `br ls --type human --priority 0 --status open` in Step 0. The actual command is `br list`, not `br ls`. Found during the bd-49j integration test dry-run (2026-05-03).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `br ls --type human --status open` in the autonovel repo
2. Observe clap usage help output instead of bead listing
3. Compare against `br list --type human --status open` which works correctly

## Symptom
Running the heartbeat sanity check verbatim from the SKILL.md prints clap usage help, not the bead list.

## Suspected cause
SKILL.md author confused `br` syntax with `gh ls` / shell `ls` aliases. /beads SKILL.md correctly uses `br list` everywhere.

## Source of report
Found 2026-05-03 during /heartbeat dry-run integration test in bd-49j flow.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All `br ls` instances in `.claude/skills/heartbeat/SKILL.md` replaced with `br list`
- Verification: `grep -n 'br ls' .claude/skills/heartbeat/SKILL.md` returns nothing
- No regression in other SKILL.md files
acceptance_criteria:
- All 'br ls' instances replaced with 'br list' in heartbeat SKILL.md
- grep returns nothing
- No regressions

title: spec: autonovel autonomous agent — multi-loop architecture, conditional skill firing, human-handoff system
description:
## 1. Overview

The autonomous autonovel agent is a long-running, multi-loop system that reads AO3 fanmail, accrues memory and identity changes, experiments with writing, and grows its own taste and audience over time. It runs inside the Claude Code harness as the orchestrator; in-harness subagents do prose generation. Cadence is event-driven where possible, time-paced where not, and autonomous creative work IS in scope — gated by gradient triggers (reactive) plus an anti-entropy clock (exploration), capped by per-skill rate limits, and surfaced through human-typed beads before any publish action.

This spec defines the loop topology, conditional skill-firing rules, the human-handoff protocol (human beads), the self-recognition guard against treating own comments as new mail, the multi-fandom rotation architecture, and the safety / stop-condition surface.

**In scope:**
- Multi-loop architecture (heartbeat + event-triggered branches)
- Routing layer that picks at most one downstream skill per heartbeat tick
- Self-recognition (filter pen-name's own comments out of "mail")
- Human-handoff system (typed `human` beads with attention markers; agent never closes them)
- Memory-growth and identity-evolution feedback path (gradient-driven /learn)
- Autonomous /conceive and /write under gradient + anti-entropy conditions
- Multi-fandom rotation (`identity/fandoms/{slug}.md` per fandom, /ssot fandom-axis on entropy fires)
- /ssot as a mandatory dependency for anti-entropy /conceive
- Stop conditions, error budgets, observability via on-disk artifacts

**NOT in scope (this spec):**
- The full implementation of bd-3kw (prompt evolution) — its own bead; this spec defines the integration point
- The full implementation of bd-y3a (fandom strategy) — its own bead; /strategy is referenced as a deferred new skill
- A web dashboard or external observability
- Multi-pen-name identity (single pen name `maren_eurynome` for now)
- /schedule-cron / cloud-durable scheduling (DECIDED OQ-01: session-bound /loop only)

## 2. Current State / Baseline

**Skills available today:**
- `/conceive` — produces `briefs/{slug}.json`, opens experiment bead
- `/write` (post-bd-bzq) — orchestrator-driven; dispatches in-harness subagents for draft/muse/revision via `write/prompts.py` builders wrapped in `wrap_for_subagent` (or `wrap_for_subagent_structured` for muse seeds)
- `/feedback` — scrapes AO3 metrics + comments via `api/ao3_client.py`, parses into `feedback/{wid}_digest.json`
- `/learn` — reads digest, edits identity files in-place, closes experiment bead
- `/daily` — five-step ritual (sanity → morning pages → mail → optional small task → EOD note → ScheduleWakeup)

**Observability artifacts on disk:**
- `feedback/*_digest.json` per published work
- `feedback/daily-log.md` (created by /daily Step 4)
- `write/runs/{run_id}/draft.md` per draft
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` task state
- `identity/{self,soul,inspirations,pen_name}.md`, `identity/voice_priors.json`, `identity/fandom_context.md`, `identity/few_shot_bank.md`

**Cadence sources available:**
- `ScheduleWakeup` — clamped to [60s, 3600s]; session-bound (DECIDED: this is the only cadence we use)
- `/loop` — repeating prompt at fixed interval; session-bound

**Currently published:**
- One work: "What the Hands Remember" (id 82950256, fandom Baldur's Gate, posted 2026-04-12). 2 kudos / 69 hits / 1 bookmark / 1 reader comment + 1 reply from `maren_eurynome` as of 2026-04-26.

**Gaps this spec fills:**
- No /heartbeat router; /daily is a single rhythm
- No self-recognition (the agent could mistake its own replies for new mail — incident logged 2026-04-26)
- No human-handoff bead type
- No gradient-driven autonomous creative firing
- No anti-entropy mechanism to fight cold-audience drift
- No multi-fandom architecture (single fandom_context.md hardcoded for BG3)

## 3. Changes and Decisions

### 3.1 Multi-loop topology — heartbeat + event-triggered branches

**Change:** One heartbeat skill plus event-driven branches, all session-bound via `/loop /heartbeat`.

- **`/heartbeat`** (NEW): runs every ≤1h via `/loop` self-paced ScheduleWakeup. Lightweight. Checks signals (mail, gradients, entropy clock, day-rollover, stale beads), runs the routing table, dispatches at most ONE downstream skill, sleeps.
- **`/daily`** (existing): fires from heartbeat once per UTC day if `feedback/daily-log.md` lacks today's line.
- **`/mail`** (NEW): fires when new non-self comments are detected. Updates digest, drafts reply via subagent, files P1 human bead.
- **`/conceive`** (existing, gains autonomous fire conditions per 3.4)
- **`/write`** (existing, gains autonomous fire conditions per 3.4)
- **`/learn`** (existing, gains autonomous fire conditions per 3.4)
- **`/strategy`** (NEW, deferred until bd-y3a + multi-piece engagement history): fandom trends + gap analysis + recommendation as P2 human bead.
- **`/triage` / `/housekeeping`**: weekly cadence from heartbeat (Sunday OR every 7 dry ticks).

**Why:** Single daily clock is too coarse for event-driven work. Heartbeat + branches correlates work with signal.

**How:** Heartbeat body is the routing decision tree (Section 4.1). Loop is permanent session-bound; closing the Claude Code session stops the loop, which is the human-attention check (per OQ-01 + OQ-23).

### 3.2 Self-recognition — `identity/handles.json`

**Change:** Structured list of pen-name AO3 handles. Every comment-stream consumer filters by this list.

- **New file:** `identity/handles.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "ao3_handles": ["maren_eurynome"],
    "display_names": ["Maren Solaire"],
    "updated_at": "2026-05-02"
  }
  ```
- **New helper:** `identity/handles.py:is_self(author: str) -> bool`
- **Default behavior:** `api/ao3_client.py:get_comments` adds kwarg `filter_self: bool = True`. ALL callers leave default ON unless explicitly auditing self-replies (e.g., reply quality QA).

**Why:** The 2026-04-26 incident — `maren_eurynome`'s reply to TheIcyQueen was nearly flagged as fresh mail. Catastrophic loop bug if not preempted.

**How:** `identity/handles.py` is a pure module loaded once per heartbeat. `get_comments` wraps the result. New AO3 handles are added by hand only (deliberate identity drift).

### 3.3 Human-handoff system — typed `human` beads

**Change:** New bead type `human` for items that require Andrew to act.

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `--type` | `human` |
| `--priority` | P0 (critical attention) / P1 (post this) / P2 (review this) |
| Title | `human: <verb> — <one-line context>` (e.g., `human: post reply — TheIcyQueen on What the Hands Remember`) |
| `--description` | Action + artifact path + deadline if any |
| `--external-ref` | AO3 URL when relevant |
| `--acceptance-criteria` | What "done" means (posted / reviewed / decided) |

**Lifecycle:**
- Agent **creates and populates** human beads.
- Agent **NEVER** closes a human bead, never sets status, never updates --status field.
- Human runs `br close <id>` after acting.
- Heartbeat surfaces open human beads at every iteration via the status-line prefix.

**Status line convention** (interactive sessions only): every orchestrator reply opens with one line summarizing loop state:
```
📬 N human · ⏰ next wake in Xm · 🔄 last heartbeat Ym ago
```
Where N is open `--type human` count.

**Why:** AO3 TOS forbids automated posting; the agent prepares, the human posts. One-way doors require explicit handoffs.

### 3.4 Routing table

Heartbeat runs this in priority order. **At most ONE skill fires per tick.** Skipped branches re-evaluate next tick.

| Priority | Signal | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Open human beads | `len(br ls --type human --status open) > 0` | **Surface only**, no skill fire |
| 2 | New non-self comment | `len(filter_self(comments_since_baseline)) > 0` | `/mail` |
| 3 | New comment + /learn-eligible | comment present AND no /learn cycle since for that work | `/learn` (NB: rate-unrestricted per OQ-19) |
| 4 | Day rollover | `today not in feedback/daily-log.md` | `/daily` |
| 5 | Brief drafted, no draft yet | brief exists + experiment bead in_progress + no `draft.md` + `last_write_at < now-48h` | `/write` |
| 6 | Reactive /conceive | last /learn closed + ≥48h since last publish + no in_progress experiment + `last_conceive_at < now-24h` | `/conceive` (informed by latest /learn) |
| 7 | Anti-entropy /conceive | entropy threshold crossed (see 3.9) AND `last_conceive_at < now-24h` | `/conceive` with mandatory `/ssot` (multi-axis incl. fandom) |
| 8 | Stale beads | `br stale --days 14 \| wc -l ≥ 5` | `/triage` |
| 9 | Weekly housekeeping due | day-of-week == Sunday AND not done this week | `/housekeeping` |
| 10 | Else | nothing | sleep (ScheduleWakeup) |

**Rate-limit gates** are AND-ed into each gradient row above. Gates live in `feedback/heartbeat-state.json` (Section 4.4).

**Identity-mutation discipline** (unchanged from spec author's intent):
- Identity files (`self.md`, `voice_priors.json`, `soul.md`, `inspirations.md`) are mutated ONLY through `/learn`. Heartbeat fires `/learn`; `/learn` does the editing; identity-mutation outside that path is a corruption signal that triggers Section 3.7 hard halt.
- `identity/handles.json` and `identity/fandoms/*.md` mutate by hand (deliberate drift).
- `identity/few_shot_bank.md` mutates via `/learn` OR `/daily` Step 3 maintenance.

### 3.5 Gradient triggers — autonomous creative firing

**Change:** `/learn`, `/conceive`, `/write` are now first-class branches in the routing table (Section 3.4). Each has explicit gradient conditions.

| Skill | Reactive trigger | Anti-entropy trigger | Rate cap |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/learn` | ANY new non-self comment since last cycle on that work | n/a (reactive only) | unrestricted |
| `/conceive` | last /learn closed + ≥48h since last publish + no in_progress experiment | entropy clock crosses adaptive threshold (3.9) | 1 per 24h |
| `/write` | brief exists + bead in_progress + no draft.md | n/a (always reactive to a brief) | 1 per 48h |

**Slop-prevention spine:**
- Every autonomous creative fire creates a P2 `human` bead with the artifact path.
- Human reviews; closes with `posted` / `won't post` / `revise` / etc.
- No autonomous publish — ever.
- /ssot mandatory on anti-entropy /conceive (per 3.6).

**Why:** Tiny audience may go silent for weeks. Gradient-only firing leaves the agent cold; anti-entropy lets the agent grow when external signal is dry. Caps prevent slop floods.

### 3.6 /ssot — mandatory dependency for anti-entropy

**Change:** Every anti-entropy `/conceive` MUST run `/ssot` first to defeat distribution collapse.

- /ssot generates a hex seed and forces choices on a list of axes (opener, metaphor, structure, rhythm, POV, climax, **fandom** — added per 3.8).
- The chosen axes become the brief's scaffolding; /conceive writes against the forced choices.
- Provenance block goes in the brief JSON (`ssot_provenance` field) so reviewers can verify the arithmetic.

**Why:** Without /ssot, every entropy /conceive collapses to "another quiet BG3 character study." That's distribution collapse, not exploration.

**Optional but recommended** for human-triggered /conceive (especially on a new fandom). Not required for /write (the brief is the constraint) or /learn (it's reactive editing, not generation).

### 3.7 Stop conditions and error budgets

**Hard halt** (orchestrator emits a P0 human bead and stops scheduling wakes):
- 3 consecutive heartbeat errors (uncaught exception, non-zero exit)
- AO3 rate-limit hit (HTTP 429) — back off entirely until human re-arms
- Identity-file mutation detected outside `/learn` (corruption signal)
- `br doctor` reports DB/JSONL drift the loop can't auto-repair
- The literal file `.heartbeat-stop` exists in repo root

**Soft halt** (skip iteration, log, keep going):
- AO3 transient error (timeout, 5xx)
- Single subagent dispatch failure

**User stop:** create `-t human -p 0 "STOP"` bead OR `touch .heartbeat-stop`. Heartbeat checks both at Step 0.

**Re-arm:** human deletes `.heartbeat-stop` OR closes the P0 STOP bead AND deletes `feedback/heartbeat-state.json`'s `consecutive_errors` field (or just the file — heartbeat will rebuild it).

### 3.8 Multi-fandom architecture

**Change:** OQ-21 promoted multi-fandom from P3-deferred-future to P1-in-scope.

- **New directory:** `identity/fandoms/` — one `{slug}.md` per fandom. Migrate existing `identity/fandom_context.md` to `identity/fandoms/bg3.md`.
- **`identity/self.md` extensions:**
  - `currently_writing_in: <slug>` (a single fandom slug; rotates over time)
  - `fandom_history: [<slug>, ...]` (chronological)
  - `fandoms_explored: {bg3: {first_seen: 2026-03-27, works_published: 1}, ...}` (per-fandom metadata)
- **/ssot fandom-axis:** the anti-entropy /conceive's forced-choice list includes a `fandom` axis. Candidates are drawn from a curated pool (`identity/fandoms/CANDIDATES.md` — list of fandoms the agent has consented to potentially explore, populated by hand). When the SSoT axis lands on a fandom NOT in `fandom_history`, the resulting brief is a "first-touch" piece — small, low-stakes, calibration-style.
- **`fandom_context.md` -> `fandoms/{slug}.md`:** existing `assemble_context` in `write/context.py` reads the fandom file based on `currently_writing_in` (or the brief's `fandom` field, if set).

**Why:** Per user — "we need to have systems that encourage exploring and writing into other fanfics, so we can experiment with more audiences and develop a style." Without multi-fandom, anti-entropy depletes within BG3 fast.

**Migration:** one bead's worth of work. `identity/fandom_context.md` becomes `identity/fandoms/bg3.md` (rename, no content change). `identity/self.md` gains the new fields. Tests in `tests/test_identity.py` updated.

### 3.9 Anti-entropy clock

**Change:** Adaptive threshold for "entropy state."

**State persisted in `feedback/heartbeat-state.json`:**
- `last_creative_fire_at` — timestamp of the most recent /conceive, /write, OR /learn (any of them resets the clock)
- `last_non_self_comment_at` — timestamp of the most recent new non-self comment across all works
- `comment_intervals_observed` — list of inter-comment-interval timedeltas, capped at 50 entries

**Computed each heartbeat:**
- `silence = max(now - last_creative_fire_at, now - last_non_self_comment_at)` (whichever is fresher resets the clock — engagement OR our own activity)
- `threshold = max(72h, 2 × median(comment_intervals_observed))`
- `entropy_state = silence > threshold`

**Cold start:** until ≥5 inter-comment intervals have been observed, threshold defaults to 72h flat.

**Why:** Static threshold misfits actual reader cadence. Sparse audience → larger gap before entropy fires (don't panic-fire). Engaged audience → faster anti-entropy (don't go cold while readers are warming up).

### 3.10 Observability

Three on-disk artifacts give the human a continuous read on the loop:
1. `feedback/daily-log.md` — one line per day from /daily Step 4
2. `feedback/heartbeat-log.md` — one line per heartbeat: timestamp + decision (e.g., `2026-05-02T18:00Z heartbeat → /mail (1 new comment)`)
3. `br ls --type human --status open` — pending human-actionable items

Plus the status-line convention (3.3) for interactive sessions.

**No web dashboard** in scope; the human reads files and runs `br` commands.

## 4. Formal Specification

### 4.1 `/heartbeat` skill (NEW)

**Inputs:** none (self-paced via `/loop /heartbeat`)
**Outputs:** zero or one skill invocation; one line appended to `feedback/heartbeat-log.md`; ScheduleWakeup at end.

```
def heartbeat():
    # Step 0: sanity + stop checks
    if exists('.heartbeat-stop'): halt('user stop file')
    state = load_or_init('feedback/heartbeat-state.json')
    if state.consecutive_errors >= 3: halt('error budget exceeded')
    if not safe_git_state(): halt('unexpected git state')
    if has_open_p0_human_bead('STOP'): halt('p0 STOP bead open')

    # Step 1: gather signals
    today = utc_today()
    daily_done = today_in(read('feedback/daily-log.md'))

    new_mail = []
    ao3_calls = 0
    for digest_path in glob('feedback/*_digest.json'):
        if ao3_calls >= 5: break  # OQ-04 cap
        wid = parse_id(digest_path)
        digest = read_digest(digest_path)
        if digest.last_fetched_at and (now - digest.last_fetched_at) < 30min:
            continue  # OQ-04 TTL
        comments = ao3.get_comments(wid)  # filter_self=True default
        ao3_calls += 1
        if not digest.baseline_at:
            # OQ-05: first-scrape silent baseline
            digest.baseline_at = now
            digest.comments = [comment_to_record(c) for c in comments]
            digest.last_fetched_at = now
            write_digest(wid, digest)
            continue
        last_seen_ids = {c.id for c in digest.comments}
        new = [c for c in comments if c.id not in last_seen_ids]
        if new:
            new_mail.extend([(wid, c) for c in new])
        digest.last_fetched_at = now
        write_digest(wid, digest)

    open_human = br_list(type='human', status='open')
    learn_pending = check_learn_eligibility()  # any work with new comment + no /learn since
    brief_ready = check_brief_ready()  # brief exists + bead in_progress + no draft.md
    conceive_reactive = check_conceive_reactive(state)  # last /learn closed + ≥48h since publish + no in_progress
    entropy = compute_entropy(state)  # 3.9 — silence > adaptive threshold
    stale_count = br_stale(days=14)
    weekly_due = (today.weekday == 6) and not housekeeping_done_this_week(state)

    # Step 2: status line for interactive sessions
    status_line(open_human=len(open_human), next_wake_secs=3500, last_heartbeat=state.last_heartbeat_at)

    # Step 3: route — at most ONE action per tick
    fire = None
    if open_human:
        log(f'surface: {len(open_human)} human beads')
    elif new_mail:
        fire = ('/mail', new_mail)
    elif learn_pending:
        fire = ('/learn', learn_pending)
    elif not daily_done:
        fire = ('/daily', None)
    elif brief_ready and (now - state.last_write_at) > 48h:
        fire = ('/write', brief_ready)
    elif conceive_reactive and (now - state.last_conceive_at) > 24h:
        fire = ('/conceive', {'mode': 'reactive'})
    elif entropy and (now - state.last_conceive_at) > 24h:
        fire = ('/conceive', {'mode': 'entropy', 'ssot_required': True})
    elif stale_count >= 5:
        fire = ('/triage', None)
    elif weekly_due:
        fire = ('/housekeeping', None)
    else:
        log('idle')

    # Step 4: invoke (with error capture)
    if fire:
        try:
            invoke_skill(*fire)
            state.consecutive_errors = 0
            update_last_fire_timestamps(state, fire)
        except Exception as e:
            state.consecutive_errors += 1
            log(f'error: {e}')

    # Step 5: persist state and schedule next wake
    state.last_heartbeat_at = now
    write_state('feedback/heartbeat-state.json', state)
    append_line('feedback/heartbeat-log.md', f'{now.isoformat()} → {fire[0] if fire else "idle"}')

    if state.consecutive_errors < 3:
        ScheduleWakeup(
            delaySeconds=clamp(3500 + jitter(±300), 60, 3600),  # ~1h with jitter
            prompt='/loop /heartbeat',
            reason=f'next heartbeat after {fire[0] if fire else "idle"}',
        )
```

**Single-skill-per-tick** is load-bearing: caps per-iteration cost. Skipped branches re-evaluate next iteration.

### 4.2 `/mail` skill (NEW)

**Inputs:** `[(work_id, Comment), ...]` from heartbeat.
**Outputs:** updated digest, queued reply files, P1 human beads.

```
def mail(items):
    for wid, comment in items:
        if is_self(comment.author):
            continue  # paranoid double-check
        if reply_already_queued(wid, comment.id):
            continue  # dedupe — heartbeat may re-see same comment until human posts

        # Update digest
        digest = read_digest(wid)
        digest.comments.append(comment_to_record(comment))
        digest.last_updated = now()
        write_digest(wid, digest)

        # Draft reply via in-harness subagent
        system = build_reply_system(
            persona=read('identity/pen_name.md'),  # Interaction Style + voice
            no_em_dashes=True,
            match_register=comment.body,
        )
        user = build_reply_user(
            work_title=work_title(wid),
            commenter=comment.author,
            comment_body=comment.body,
        )
        prompt = wrap_for_subagent_structured(
            system=system,
            user=user,
            output_kind='a single AO3 reply, plain text, no preamble, ≤300 words',
        )
        reply_text = dispatch_subagent('general-purpose', prompt)

        # Save reply + file human bead
        path = f'publish/replies_queue/{wid}_{comment.id}.md'
        write_file(path, reply_text)
        bead_id = br_create(
            type='human',
            priority=1,
            title=f'human: post reply — {comment.author} on {work_title(wid)}',
            description=(
                f'New comment from {comment.author} on AO3 work {wid}.\n'
                f'Reply drafted at {path}.\n'
                f'Comment body:\n\n{comment.body}\n\n'
                f'Action: review the reply, edit if needed, post on AO3.'
            ),
            external_ref=ao3_comment_url(wid, comment.id),
            acceptance_criteria=(
                '- [ ] Reply reviewed\n'
                '- [ ] Reply posted on AO3\n'
                '- [ ] Bead closed by Andrew (NEVER by agent)'
            ),
        )
```

**Note:** /mail does NOT close any bead. The orchestrator (heartbeat) does not close human beads. Only Andrew closes.

### 4.3 `/learn` autonomous integration

`/learn` SKILL.md remains as-is for the editing logic. Heartbeat passes the work_id and comment delta. Inside the skill, the orchestrator reads the digest, identifies what's new, edits identity files in place, and closes the experiment bead per existing semantics. The autonomy added by this spec is purely the firing condition (Section 3.4 row 3) — not the skill body.

### 4.4 `feedback/heartbeat-state.json` schema

```json
{
  "last_heartbeat_at": "ISO-8601",
  "last_creative_fire_at": "ISO-8601 | null",
  "last_non_self_comment_at": "ISO-8601 | null",
  "last_write_at": "ISO-8601 | null",
  "last_conceive_at": "ISO-8601 | null",
  "last_learn_at": "ISO-8601 | null",
  "comment_intervals_observed": ["PT3D5H", "PT12H", ...],
  "consecutive_errors": 0,
  "housekeeping_done_week": "2026-W18",
  "ao3_calls_today": 0
}
```

`last_creative_fire_at` resets when ANY of /conceive, /write, /learn fire (whichever is most recent). The narrower per-skill timestamps drive rate-limit gates.

### 4.5 `identity/handles.json` schema and helper

```json
{
  "ao3_handles": ["maren_eurynome"],
  "display_names": ["Maren Solaire"],
  "updated_at": "ISO-8601"
}
```

```python
# identity/handles.py
import json
from pathlib import Path

_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / 'handles.json'

def is_self(author: str) -> bool:
    data = json.loads(_PATH.read_text())
    return author in data['ao3_handles']
```

### 4.6 Human-bead protocol

```
agent creates: br create -t human -p {0,1,2} "human: <verb> — <ctx>"
agent populates: --description, --external-ref, --acceptance-criteria
agent NEVER: br update --status, br close, br defer
human acts: posts the reply / reviews the draft / decides
human runs: br close <id>
```

Agent commit messages MAY reference the human bead (`Bead: <id>` trailer) but the agent's commit does NOT close it.

### 4.7 Multi-fandom file layout

```
identity/
  self.md                      # adds currently_writing_in, fandom_history, fandoms_explored
  pen_name.md
  voice_priors.json
  soul.md
  inspirations.md
  few_shot_bank.md
  handles.json                 # NEW (3.2)
  fandoms/                     # NEW
    bg3.md                     # was: identity/fandom_context.md
    CANDIDATES.md              # NEW: list of fandoms the agent may explore via /ssot fandom-axis
```

`write/context.py:assemble_context` reads `identity/fandoms/{currently_writing_in}.md` (or `{brief.fandom}` if the brief sets it explicitly).

## 5. Test Cases

```
TEST: heartbeat-empty-state
INPUT: Fresh repo. No new mail. Daily done. No stale beads. No human beads. No briefs in flight.
EXPECTED: routes to "idle". ScheduleWakeup called. heartbeat-log.md gains one line.
RATIONALE: idle path is the most common case; must be cheap and quiet.

TEST: heartbeat-self-comment-filtered
INPUT: AO3 returns one comment from `maren_eurynome` and one from `TheIcyQueen`. Digest has TheIcyQueen.
EXPECTED: filter_self drops maren_eurynome. Diff finds zero new. No /mail invocation. Logs idle.
RATIONALE: 2026-04-26 incident — must not treat own replies as new mail.

TEST: heartbeat-new-reader-comment
INPUT: AO3 returns a comment from `new_reader_42` not in the digest.
EXPECTED: /mail fires. Digest updated. publish/replies_queue/{wid}_{cid}.md written. P1 human bead created (type=human, priority=1).
RATIONALE: Happy-path mail flow.

TEST: heartbeat-baseline-on-first-scrape
INPUT: Brand-new work_id — no digest exists yet. AO3 returns 5 comments (some pre-existing readers, some not).
EXPECTED: Digest written with all 5 comments and baseline_at=now. NO /mail invocation. NO human beads.
RATIONALE: OQ-05 — first-scrape silent baseline avoids mass-mail event on digest wipe / new work.

TEST: heartbeat-day-rollover
INPUT: feedback/daily-log.md has yesterday's date but not today's. No mail. No brief in flight.
EXPECTED: /daily fires.
RATIONALE: Daily ritual triggers exactly once per UTC day.

TEST: heartbeat-day-already-done
INPUT: feedback/daily-log.md has today's date. No mail. No human beads.
EXPECTED: routes to idle. No /daily fire.
RATIONALE: Same-day repeat wakes (1h ScheduleWakeup clamp) must short-circuit.

TEST: heartbeat-write-fires-when-brief-ready
INPUT: Brief at briefs/foo.json. Experiment bead bd-XYZ in_progress. No write/runs/foo/draft.md. last_write_at = 72h ago.
EXPECTED: /write fires.
RATIONALE: Brief-driven gradient (3.4 row 5).

TEST: heartbeat-write-rate-limited
INPUT: Same as above but last_write_at = 24h ago.
EXPECTED: /write does NOT fire. Heartbeat moves to next branch.
RATIONALE: 48h cap (OQ-19).

TEST: heartbeat-conceive-reactive
INPUT: Last /learn closed yesterday. Last publish 5 days ago. No in_progress experiment. last_conceive_at = 30h ago.
EXPECTED: /conceive fires in mode=reactive (no /ssot required).
RATIONALE: Reactive /conceive after /learn surfaces a direction.

TEST: heartbeat-conceive-entropy
INPUT: No new mail in 4 days. No creative fire in 4 days. Cold start (no historical comment intervals).
EXPECTED: silence (96h) > threshold (72h). entropy_state=True. /conceive fires in mode=entropy with ssot_required=True.
RATIONALE: Anti-entropy fights cold-audience drift.

TEST: heartbeat-conceive-entropy-uses-ssot
INPUT: As above. Inspect the dispatched /conceive prompt.
EXPECTED: /ssot is invoked first. Resulting brief contains an ssot_provenance field with seed and forced-choice arithmetic.
RATIONALE: OQ-22 — /ssot mandatory on entropy fires; otherwise distribution collapse.

TEST: heartbeat-stop-file
INPUT: .heartbeat-stop exists in repo root.
EXPECTED: heartbeat halts at Step 0. NO ScheduleWakeup. Logs the halt.
RATIONALE: Human kill-switch must work.

TEST: heartbeat-error-budget
INPUT: heartbeat-state.json shows consecutive_errors=2. Skill dispatch raises.
EXPECTED: consecutive_errors increments to 3. Next heartbeat halts. P0 human bead "STOP — error budget exceeded" created.
RATIONALE: Halt prevents runaway-error loops.

TEST: heartbeat-rate-limit-halt
INPUT: AO3 returns HTTP 429.
EXPECTED: Hard halt. P0 human bead created. NO ScheduleWakeup until human re-arms.
RATIONALE: AO3 account-level rate-limit prevention.

TEST: heartbeat-ao3-call-cap
INPUT: 10 published works, all with stale digests (>30min).
EXPECTED: Only first 5 fetched. heartbeat-state.json's ao3_calls_today incremented to 5. Remaining 5 deferred to next heartbeat.
RATIONALE: OQ-04 cap.

TEST: heartbeat-ao3-ttl
INPUT: Digest's last_fetched_at = 15min ago.
EXPECTED: That work skipped this iteration. No AO3 call.
RATIONALE: OQ-04 TTL.

TEST: human-bead-not-auto-closed
INPUT: /mail creates human bead bd-XYZ. Heartbeat fires 50 times.
EXPECTED: bd-XYZ remains open across all 50 ticks. Agent never calls br close on it.
RATIONALE: Human beads are one-way handoffs (3.3).

TEST: human-bead-status-surfaces
INPUT: 3 open human beads. Interactive session.
EXPECTED: Heartbeat status-line prefix shows "📬 3 human ...".
RATIONALE: Observability (3.10).

TEST: mail-dedupe
INPUT: Comment from new_reader_42 already has a queued reply at publish/replies_queue/{wid}_{cid}.md AND an open human bead. Heartbeat re-fetches and sees the comment again.
EXPECTED: /mail does NOT create a duplicate reply file or duplicate human bead.
RATIONALE: Heartbeat re-fires until human closes the bead; without dedupe, queue spam.

TEST: identity-files-untouched-by-loop
INPUT: 100 simulated heartbeats over a week WITHOUT any /learn fire (e.g., no new comments).
EXPECTED: identity/{self,voice_priors,soul,inspirations,few_shot_bank,handles}.* unchanged. identity/fandoms/*.md unchanged.
RATIONALE: Identity mutation is /learn-only; outside that path is corruption.

TEST: ssot-axis-includes-fandom
INPUT: Anti-entropy /conceive dispatches with ssot_required=True. fandom_history = [bg3]. CANDIDATES.md lists [bg3, mass_effect, dragon_age].
EXPECTED: /ssot's forced-choice axes include `fandom`, candidate set drawn from CANDIDATES.md, hex-arithmetic determines the pick. Result may be bg3 (continuation) or any other (first-touch).
RATIONALE: 3.8 — multi-fandom exploration.

TEST: entropy-adaptive-threshold
INPUT: comment_intervals_observed = [PT12H, PT24H, PT24H, PT36H, PT48H, PT72H]. Median = PT30H. silence = PT60H.
EXPECTED: threshold = max(72h, 2*30h) = 72h. silence < threshold → entropy_state=False.
RATIONALE: 3.9 — adaptive threshold.

TEST: heartbeat-log-append-only
INPUT: Run heartbeat 10 times.
EXPECTED: feedback/heartbeat-log.md gains exactly 10 lines. Earlier lines unchanged.
RATIONALE: Observability artifact must be reliable.

TEST: heartbeat-skill-priority
INPUT: Both new mail AND a brief ready AND stale beads (≥5).
EXPECTED: /mail fires (highest gradient priority after human-bead-surface). Brief and triage deferred to next iteration.
RATIONALE: Single-skill-per-tick + priority order (3.4).

TEST: heartbeat-no-conceive-during-active-experiment
INPUT: An experiment bead is in_progress. last_conceive_at = 30h ago (would otherwise satisfy gate).
EXPECTED: Reactive /conceive does NOT fire. Branch skipped (condition explicitly checks for no in_progress experiment).
RATIONALE: Don't conceive a new piece while one is in flight.

TEST: heartbeat-conceive-entropy-respects-rate-cap
INPUT: entropy_state=True. last_conceive_at = 18h ago.
EXPECTED: /conceive does NOT fire. Heartbeat continues to next branch.
RATIONALE: Rate cap (OQ-19) applies to anti-entropy fires too.
```

## 6. Open Questions

All P1, P2, P3 OQs from the original spec walk plus added OQ-16 through OQ-23 are DECIDED. See bd-49j --notes for the full decision trail with rationale and spec-impact line per item.

Status summary:
- **OQ-01:** DECIDED — session-bound /loop only, never /schedule cron.
- **OQ-02:** MOOT — superseded by OQ-01 (no session = loop dies, by design).
- **OQ-03:** DECIDED — reuse `wrap_for_subagent_structured` for replies.
- **OQ-04:** DECIDED — per-work TTL 30min + cap 5 AO3 calls per heartbeat.
- **OQ-05:** DECIDED — baseline-on-first-scrape (no flag).
- **OQ-06:** MOOT — subsumed by OQ-05.
- **OQ-07:** DEFERRED to P3 — engagement-spike heuristic; revisit when bd-pc8 ships.
- **OQ-08:** DECIDED — status line collapses to `📬 N pending`.
- **OQ-09:** DECIDED — no cross-work mail dedupe; each (work × comment) is its own bead.
- **OQ-10:** SUPERSEDED by OQ-16 — /learn auto-fires now.
- **OQ-11:** OVERRIDDEN by OQ-19 — drafting is in the loop with caps.
- **OQ-12:** SUPERSEDED by OQ-21 — multi-fandom now P1 in scope.
- **OQ-13:** DECIDED — terse machine logs.
- **OQ-14:** DECIDED — no `br stats` dump per tick (/housekeeping handles).
- **OQ-15:** DEFERRED — only specialize subagent type if persona-leak observed.
- **OQ-16:** DECIDED — /learn fires on ANY new non-self comment.
- **OQ-17:** DECIDED — /conceive: reactive (post-/learn + 48h since publish + no in-flight) OR entropy (clock crossed).
- **OQ-18:** DECIDED — /write: brief exists + bead in_progress + no draft.md + 48h cap.
- **OQ-19:** DECIDED — caps: 1 /write per 48h, 1 /conceive per 24h, /learn unrestricted, no auto-publish, every fire = P2 human bead.
- **OQ-20:** DECIDED — adaptive entropy threshold = max(72h, 2× median inter-comment interval).
- **OQ-21:** DECIDED — multi-fandom IN scope. identity/fandoms/{slug}.md per fandom.
- **OQ-22:** DECIDED — /ssot mandatory on anti-entropy /conceive.
- **OQ-23:** REJECTED — no backpressure mechanism. Session presence is the human-attention check.

**Net new OQs surfaced during /check:** none beyond OQ-16 through OQ-23.
**Conflicts:** none.
**Cross-spec deps:** /ssot global skill (verified exists). bd-y3a (deferred) provides /strategy MVP. bd-3kw (deferred) provides /evolve MVP.

## 7. Future Considerations

- **Multi-pen-name identity.** Once voice consistency and reputation are earned, a second pen name (different fandom register) is a natural evolution.
- **Cross-fandom reader memory.** Per-reader profile across works (engagement style, history of interactions) for reply personalization.
- **Engagement attribution.** Correlate kudos/hit spikes to events (rec posts, tag trends) — surfaces what works.
- **/evolve (prompt evolution).** Once bd-3kw lands, the loop can autonomously run held-out evaluations and propose system-prompt mutations. Mutations to identity files still require human approval.
- **/strategy (fandom analysis).** Once bd-y3a lands and there's enough engagement data (≥3 published pieces with feedback cycles), monthly /strategy fires and surfaces a P2 human bead with a recommended next direction.
- **Editorial sleep cycles.** Periods where the agent stops drafting and only reads (intentional fallow). Mirrors how human writers operate. Triggered by human or by a "diminishing returns" signal in /learn cycles.
- **Public observability.** Read-only dashboard at a personal URL showing loop state, recent decisions, pending human beads. Outside scope but a natural extension once the loop is stable.
- **Cross-fandom voice studies.** When the agent has 3+ published works in two fandoms, compare voice metrics across them — does Maren sound like the same writer in BG3 and ME? What carries, what shifts?
acceptance_criteria:
- Section 1.1 sources documented (which docs informed this spec)
- Sections 2 and 3 cite existing code or specs by reference  
- Section 5 has >=10 test cases with INPUT/EXPECTED/RATIONALE (this spec has 14)
- Section 6 lists 15 open questions (5 P1, 6 P2, 4 P3) with recommendations
- Self-recognition guard (handles.json + is_self filter) is mandatory in any /mail or comment-stream consumer
- Human-bead protocol (-t human, agent never closes, P0/1/2 priority) defined
notes:
## Amendment 2026-05-03 — OQ-05 baseline-on-first-scrape, retrofit case (bd-uyx)

The "baseline-on-first-scrape" rule (OQ-05, Section 4.2) was designed for greenfield digests but misfits a third case the spec didn't anticipate: a digest that EXISTS but predates this spec (no `baseline_at` marker, but rich per-comment metadata from prior /learn cycles).

**Refined rule** (replaces the simple overwrite in Section 4.1 pseudocode + Section 4.2):

When `not digest.get("baseline_at")` — three sub-cases all funnel through ONE merge code path that NEVER fires /mail this iteration:

- **(a)** Brand-new digest (no comments list, or empty) — write fresh baseline from AO3 fetch.
- **(b)** Pre-bd-49j digest — preserve existing per-comment metadata (`sentiment`, `register`, `quoted_passage`, `quoted_passage_significance`, `specific_craft_named`, etc.). Merge: for each AO3 comment, if its id matches an existing record, leave that record alone; if new, append a basic record. The merged set becomes the baseline.
- **(c)** Digest wiped after corruption — same merge logic; if existing list is empty, behaves like (a).

In all three cases, set `baseline_at = now`, set `last_fetched_at = now`, write, continue. NO /mail invocation this iteration. Subsequent iterations follow the normal diff path.

**Rationale:** the existing rich metadata in pre-spec digests represents real reader-analysis work from manual /learn cycles. Overwriting it loses information we want to keep. The merge-by-id pattern preserves it without compromising the safety invariant (no mass-fire).

**Test coverage:** the heartbeat orchestrator code that implements this lives in `.claude/skills/heartbeat/SKILL.md` Step 1a (not Python), so there's no pytest harness. A future bead may extract a `feedback.heartbeat_state.merge_baseline(existing_digest, comments) -> dict` helper for testability — deferred for now; the rule is documented here and the SKILL.md was updated to match.

**Source:** found 2026-05-03 in /heartbeat dry-run integration test.

title: tests: identity/handles + is_self filter
description:
## Context

Self-recognition is the load-bearing guard against the loop treating Maren's own AO3 replies as new mail (incident 2026-04-26: nearly drafted a reply to our own comment). Spec bd-49j Section 3.2 + 4.5 define identity/handles.json and identity/handles.py:is_self.

## Task

Write tests for the new module `identity/handles.py` and its data file `identity/handles.json`. Test files go to `tests/test_handles.py`.

DO NOT create or modify implementation source files (no identity/handles.py or identity/handles.json). Tests import from real module paths and will fail at runtime until impl agent (bd-49j.5) creates them — that's correct TDD.

## Test cases to cover

From spec Section 5:
- `heartbeat-self-comment-filtered` — comment from `maren_eurynome` is_self=True; `TheIcyQueen` is_self=False

Plus edge / error cases:

- `is_self` is case-sensitive (AO3 usernames are case-sensitive in API)

## Acceptance Criteria

title: skill: /mail — react to one new comment (spec bd-49j Section 4.2)
description:
## Context

Spec bd-49j Section 4.2 defines /mail as the react-to-one-comment branch. /heartbeat invokes it when new non-self comments are detected. /mail filters self again (paranoid double-check), dedupes against existing reply queue, drafts a reply via in-harness subagent, files a P1 human bead.

## Task

Write `.claude/skills/mail/SKILL.md` per Section 4.2 + 3.3 (human-bead protocol) + reply-drafting rules (no em-dashes, match commenter's energy from pen_name.md Interaction Style).

## Acceptance Criteria

- .claude/skills/mail/SKILL.md exists
- Documents the per-comment processing pipeline: filter_self → dedupe → digest update → reply draft → queue → human bead
- Names the subagent dispatch shape (general-purpose, wrap_for_subagent_structured with output_kind="a single AO3 reply, plain text, no preamble, ≤300 words")
- Documents the human-bead spec: P1 priority, NEVER closed by agent, AO3 URL in --external-ref, action + artifact path in --description
- Anti-patterns: auto-posting, dropping comments to the floor, not deduping (creates spam), modifying digest outside the per-comment update, calling br close on the human bead
acceptance_criteria:
- .claude/skills/mail/SKILL.md exists matching spec Section 4.2
- Pipeline (filter → dedupe → digest → draft → queue → human bead) documented
- Subagent dispatch shape named precisely (wrap_for_subagent_structured)
- Human-bead protocol referenced
- Anti-patterns section

title: tests: api/ao3_client TTL freshness + filter_self kwarg
description:
## Context

Spec bd-49j OQ-04 + Section 4.1 require api/ao3_client.py to grow per-resource TTL caching (don't refetch a digest if last_fetched_at < 30min ago) and a `filter_self: bool = True` kwarg on `get_comments` that wraps results through `identity.handles.is_self`. The 5-call-per-heartbeat cap is tracked in heartbeat-state, NOT in ao3_client itself — out of this bead's scope.

## Task

Write tests for the additions to `api/ao3_client.py`. Test file: `tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py` (new file; existing tests/test_api_proxy.py covers different concerns).

DO NOT create or modify any source files. Imports from `api.ao3_client` should reference real symbols even if not yet present.

## Test cases to cover

From spec Section 5:
- `heartbeat-ao3-ttl` — digest's last_fetched_at = 15min ago → that work skipped (no AO3 call)
- `heartbeat-self-comment-filtered` — `get_comments(wid)` with default `filter_self=True` drops self-authored comments

Plus edge / error cases:
- `get_comments(wid, filter_self=False)` returns ALL comments including self-authored (audit/QA path)
- TTL boundary: last_fetched_at exactly 30min ago → fetch (or skip — pick a precise rule and test it)
- TTL with no last_fetched_at field → fetch (cold start)
- Existing rate-limited single-request behavior unaffected (3s/req throttle still works)
- TTL check is in a wrapper / helper, not buried in `_get` (so heartbeat can inspect digest freshness without making a request)

Use mock httpx responses (existing `tests/test_api_proxy.py` likely has fixtures); do not hit live AO3 in tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py` exists
- ≥7 test functions
- Tests use mocked httpx (no real network calls)
- Imports `from api.ao3_client import get_comments` (existing) and any new helper exposed for TTL freshness check (impl agent decides the name; test against the contract: "given a digest path, return whether to refetch")
- All tests deterministically pass once impl lands

## Files

- Create: `tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py`
- Reference: `api/ao3_client.py`, `tests/test_api_proxy.py`, `api/models.py`
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py exists with >=7 tests; mocked httpx (no live AO3); covers TTL freshness + filter_self default-on + audit-mode false; existing tests/test_api_proxy.py untouched

title: tests: feedback/heartbeat-state schema + entropy clock + rate-limit gates
description:
## Context

Spec bd-49j Section 3.9 + 4.4 + Section 5 (multiple cases) define `feedback/heartbeat-state.json` as the persistence file for loop state: last_creative_fire_at, last_non_self_comment_at, per-skill rate-limit timestamps, comment_intervals_observed for adaptive entropy threshold, consecutive_errors, ao3_calls_today, etc.

## Task

Write tests for the new module that manages this state file. Module path will be `feedback/heartbeat_state.py` (impl agent creates it). Test file: `tests/test_heartbeat_state.py`.

DO NOT create or modify implementation source. Imports point at real module paths.

## Test cases to cover

From spec Section 5:
- `heartbeat-error-budget` — consecutive_errors increments to 3 → next heartbeat halts (test the state persistence + counter math; halt logic is in /heartbeat skill, not this module)
- `entropy-adaptive-threshold` — comment_intervals_observed = [PT12H, PT24H, PT24H, PT36H, PT48H, PT72H], median=PT30H → threshold = max(72h, 60h) = 72h
- `heartbeat-log-append-only` — feedback/heartbeat-log.md is append-only (this module owns the append helper)

Plus edge / error cases:
- Schema validation: load returns defaults when file missing
- Schema validation: load tolerates missing optional fields (forward-compat)
- compute_entropy(state, now) returns False when silence < threshold, True when silence > threshold
- Cold start: < 5 inter-comment intervals → threshold defaults to 72h flat
- update_last_fire(skill_name, ts) updates the right per-skill field AND last_creative_fire_at
- comment_intervals_observed capped at 50 entries (FIFO eviction)
- record_error increments consecutive_errors; record_success resets to 0
- write_state is atomic (write-temp-rename pattern; partial writes don't corrupt)
- ao3_calls_today resets at UTC midnight
- housekeeping_done_week ISO-week tracking ('2026-W18' format)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `tests/test_heartbeat_state.py` exists
- ≥12 test functions covering happy path + edge + error
- Tests use a temp dir / pytest tmp_path for the state file (no real feedback/ writes)
- Imports `from feedback.heartbeat_state import (load_state, write_state, update_last_fire, record_error, record_success, compute_entropy, ...)` — exact names left to impl
- All tests deterministically pass once impl lands

## Files

- Create: `tests/test_heartbeat_state.py`
- Reference: spec bd-49j Section 4.4 (schema), Section 3.9 (entropy clock), Section 5 (test cases)
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_heartbeat_state.py exists with >=12 tests; uses pytest tmp_path for state file; covers schema, atomic writes, entropy threshold, error budget, FIFO eviction, daily reset

title: tests: identity/fandoms migration + self.md fandom fields
description:

title: impl: api/ao3_client TTL + filter_self kwarg
description:
## Context

Spec bd-49j OQ-04 + Section 4.1 require api/ao3_client.py to support per-resource freshness checks (don't refetch <30min) and self-comment filtering on `get_comments`.

## Task

Modify `api/ao3_client.py` to satisfy `tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py`.

## Changes

1. Add `filter_self: bool = True` kwarg to `get_comments(work_id, chapter_id=None, *, filter_self=True)`. When True, wrap result through `identity.handles.is_self` and drop matches.
2. Add a freshness-check helper. Suggested signature:
   ```python
   def needs_refetch(digest_path: Path | str, ttl_seconds: int = 1800) -> bool:
       # returns True if no digest, or digest's last_fetched_at is older than ttl
   ```
   Heartbeat will call this to decide whether to fetch. The helper does NOT make AO3 calls itself.
3. Don't alter the existing 3s/req inner throttle.

## Dependencies

- This bead requires `identity/handles.py` (bd-49j.5). If bd-49j.5 hasn't merged yet, do not start. The orchestrator must merge bd-49j.5 first.

## Acceptance criteria

- `tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py` passes (do NOT modify)
- All existing tests in `tests/test_api_proxy.py` still pass
- `uv run ruff check api/ tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py` clean
- No new top-level dependencies

## Files NOT to modify

- `tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py`, `tests/test_api_proxy.py`
- Anything outside `api/`

## See

Spec bd-49j Section 4.1 (heartbeat pseudocode shows TTL + cap usage), OQ-04 decision in --notes.
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_ao3_client_ttl.py passes; existing tests/test_api_proxy.py still passes; ruff clean; no new top-level deps

title: impl: feedback/heartbeat-state module + entropy clock
description:
## Context

Spec bd-49j Section 3.9 + 4.4 + Section 5 (multiple cases) define `feedback/heartbeat-state.json` schema and the module that manages it: load/save, last-fire timestamps, entropy threshold computation, error budget.

## Task

Build `feedback/heartbeat_state.py` to satisfy `tests/test_heartbeat_state.py`.

## Module surface (names final per tests/test_heartbeat_state.py — adapt your impl to it)

Core functions:
- `load_state(path: Path) -> HeartbeatState` — returns defaults if file missing
- `write_state(state: HeartbeatState, path: Path) -> None` — atomic write (temp + rename)
- `update_last_fire(state: HeartbeatState, skill: str, ts: datetime) -> HeartbeatState`
- `record_error(state: HeartbeatState) -> HeartbeatState` (increments consecutive_errors)
- `record_success(state: HeartbeatState) -> HeartbeatState` (resets to 0)
- `compute_entropy(state: HeartbeatState, now: datetime) -> bool`
- `record_comment_interval(state: HeartbeatState, interval: timedelta) -> HeartbeatState` (FIFO cap 50)
- `append_heartbeat_log(line: str, log_path: Path) -> None`

Schema (Section 4.4 of spec):
```python
@dataclass
class HeartbeatState:
    last_heartbeat_at: datetime | None
    last_creative_fire_at: datetime | None
    last_non_self_comment_at: datetime | None
    last_write_at: datetime | None
    last_conceive_at: datetime | None
    last_learn_at: datetime | None
    comment_intervals_observed: list[timedelta]  # capped at 50
    consecutive_errors: int
    housekeeping_done_week: str | None  # ISO week, e.g. "2026-W18"
    ao3_calls_today: int
    ao3_calls_date: date | None  # for daily reset
```

JSON serialization: ISO-8601 strings; `comment_intervals_observed` as ISO-8601 durations; date as YYYY-MM-DD.

## Acceptance criteria

- `tests/test_heartbeat_state.py` passes (do NOT modify)
- `uv run ruff check feedback/ tests/test_heartbeat_state.py` clean
- Module is pure: no imports from write/, identity/, api/ (state is foundational)
- Atomic writes: never leave a half-written file on the disk

## Files NOT to modify

- `tests/test_heartbeat_state.py`
- Anything outside `feedback/`

## See

Spec bd-49j Section 3.9 (anti-entropy clock), Section 4.4 (schema), Section 5 (test cases entropy-adaptive-threshold, heartbeat-error-budget).
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_heartbeat_state.py passes; ruff clean on feedback/heartbeat_state.py; module pure (no write/identity/api imports)

title: impl: identity/fandoms migration + self.md fandom fields
description:
## Context

Spec bd-49j Section 3.8 + 4.7 promote multi-fandom from out-of-scope to in-scope. This bead does the migration: rename `identity/fandom_context.md` → `identity/fandoms/bg3.md`, create `identity/fandoms/CANDIDATES.md`, extend `identity/self.md` schema, update `write/context.py` to read the right fandom file.

## Task

Satisfy `tests/test_multi_fandom.py`.

## Changes

1. **File rename:** `git mv identity/fandom_context.md identity/fandoms/bg3.md`. `mkdir -p identity/fandoms/` first.
2. **New file:** `identity/fandoms/CANDIDATES.md` listing fandoms the agent may explore via /ssot's fandom-axis on anti-entropy /conceive. Initial content: at least bg3 plus 2-3 plausible candidates (suggest: Disco Elysium, Mass Effect, Dragon Age — adjacent in tone/audience). One per line, slug + display name.
3. **Schema additions to `identity/self.md`** front matter (or wherever the file's structured fields live — read it first):
   - `currently_writing_in: bg3`

4. **`identity/schema.py`** — extend the schema/parsing to expose these new fields on the loaded identity dict.
5. **`write/context.py:assemble_context`** — when building fandom_context, read from `identity/fandoms/{brief.fandom or identity['currently_writing_in']}.md`. Raise a clear error if the fandom file doesn't exist.

## Acceptance criteria
