4. **Persona-bound voice fingerprinting.** autonovel's `voice_fingerprint.py` and InkOS's "style fingerprint injection at the prompt level" both treat voice as a measurable artifact extracted from a corpus, not a free-form vibe in `self.md`. Our `voice_priors.json` is in the right shape; we should make sure it's *enforced* per generation, not just consulted.
5. **Multi-tier model routing per agent role.** AuthorClaw and InkOS both route different roles to different model tiers explicitly. This matches our Qwen3-Coder / Trinity-Mini / Laguna-XS.2 split.

None of these patterns *require* a specialized harness — they require disciplined skills + a truth store. Hermes Agent's skill/scheduler/multi-backend model gives us the substrate; the writing-specific patterns are content we author.

## Honorable mentions (commercial / hosted only — excluded)

- **Sudowrite** — 20+ proprietary models (Muse, Claude, GPT, Gemini); has a new Developer API in 2026 but no local-model story and no exposed daemon. Specialized for fiction but architecturally a SaaS we'd be billed per token for.
- **NovelCrafter** — *does* connect to local Ollama (`OLLAMA_ORIGINS=https://app.novelcrafter.com ollama serve`), but the app itself is a closed-source web UI. Useful as a reference for what a writer-facing UI looks like; not adoptable as substrate.
- **Plottr** — outliner with an "off by default" optional AI layer. Strictly a planning tool; not generative substrate.
- **NovelAI** — proprietary text models (Xialong 355B GLM-4.6 fine-tune, Kayra 13B); image weights are released, text weights are not.

## Recommendation

**Stay course on Hermes Agent. Do not switch substrates. Mine autonovel and InkOS for content patterns.**

Concrete next steps:

1. **Read `NousResearch/autonovel` end-to-end.** It's 3 commits and ~1k stars precisely because it's a worked example. Extract: the 4-phase pipeline shape, `voice_fingerprint.py` algorithm, the `canon.md` ↔ outline ↔ chapter bidirectional-propagation pattern, the dual-persona Opus review prompt, and the regex-then-LLM evaluation tier. Port the patterns; the Anthropic-only/no-daemon shape is exactly what we're correcting.
2. **Steal InkOS's fatigue lists and prohibited-pattern set wholesale** (AGPL-3.0 means attribution if we redistribute; we're running it internally, so we're fine — but track the provenance). Wire them into our `evaluate_fanfic` slop check as the mechanical pre-pass before any LLM judge fires. This is the highest-leverage single artifact we can lift.
3. **Adopt InkOS's "truth files" concept** as a parallel to our `self.md`/`voice_priors.json`/`few_shot_bank.md`. We have author-identity primitives; we need *fandom-canon* primitives (character matrix, ship dynamics, prior-chapter recap, current arc state) that the writer agent reads and writes on every turn. The model can't keep this in context for a 24/7 serial.
4. **Do not adopt Novel-OS, AIStoryWriter, LibriScribe, or StoryCraftr as substrate.** They are smaller, less mature, and don't give us anything Hermes can't host as skills.
5. **Tag every AO3 post `AI-assisted writing`** per AO3's stated tagging policy. AO3 doesn't ban AI-assisted work but requires disclosure when AI was used substantively; our `CLAUDE.md` "if readers suspect AI, the project is dead" constraint is about *prose quality*, not policy compliance. Both apply.

**Low-leverage research finding:** nothing in this scan competes with Hermes Agent as a substrate for a 24/7 fanfic daemon. The space is full of one-shot novel generators and demo workshops; no one has published a fanfic-specific serial daemon. We're not behind a frontier; we're building in a gap.

## Sources

- https://github.com/NousResearch/autonovel
- https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
- https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/
- https://github.com/Narcooo/inkos
- https://github.com/guerra2fernando/libriscribe
- https://github.com/mrigankad/Novel-OS
- https://github.com/datacrystals/AIStoryWriter
- https://github.com/raestrada/storycraftr
- https://github.com/Ckokoski/authorclaw
- https://github.com/adamwlarson/ai-book-writer
- https://github.com/Doriandarko/gemini-writer
- https://github.com/EdwardAThomson/NovelWriter
- https://github.com/jrrobison1/llm-writer-workshop
- https://github.com/jbpayton/robot-writers-room
- https://github.com/sharathDHD/fanfiction-novel-generator
- https://docs.sudowrite.com/
- https://docs.novelcrafter.com/en/articles/8680069-connecting-to-ollama
- https://plottr.com/ai-for-writing-uses/
- https://docs.novelai.net/en/text/models/
- https://fanlore.org/wiki/AO3_&_AI_Generated_Content
- https://fanlore.org/wiki/AI_Generated_Content

title: spec: Phase 1 minimum-viable Hermes-autonovel (D2 pivot Phase 1)
description:
# Spec: Phase 1 Minimum-Viable Hermes-Autonovel

**Bead:** bd-b5p.4 · **Phase:** 1 of 4 · **Status:** spec
**Parent:** bd-b5p (epic) · **Predecessor:** bd-b5p.2 (autonovel-on-Hermes arch research)

## 1. Overview

Prove the autonovel pipeline can run end-to-end on Hermes Agent (NousResearch) substrate without using Claude Code as the orchestrator. The smallest possible demonstration that the migration is real.

**Hypothesis (from bd-b5p.2 research):** every load-bearing autonovel primitive has a direct Hermes counterpart. The Phase 1 deliverable is to PROVE this end-to-end with one concrete pipeline run, not to migrate everything.

**Goal:** `hermes cron create '0 9 * * *' '/skill:autonovel-phase1-smoke ...'` produces one written paragraph in `write/runs/phase1-smoke/draft.md` daily.

**Out of scope (deferred to Phase 2):**
- AO3 staging integration
- Reader feedback loop
- Voice-anchor injection via few_shot_bank
- Multi-skill orchestration (just one skill in Phase 1)
- Halt-machinery integration (Hermes' built-in iteration_budget is sufficient for Phase 1)
- Custom plugins (pico-tailnet, beads-observability) — Phase 2

## 2. Current state / baseline (operational facts)

| Component | Status |
|---|---|
| Hermes Agent v0.14.0 | Installed at `/home/ubuntu/explore/hermes-agent-trial/.venv` |
| Hermes config | `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (custom provider, qwen3-coder:30b Ollama) |
| End-to-end tool-use | ✅ verified via probe (hermes -z 'Use the bash tool to cat /tmp/...' returns file contents) |
| autonovel write/prompts.py | Unchanged from current Claude Code in-harness path |
| autonovel identity/* | Unchanged (self.md, voice_priors.json, few_shot_bank.md, etc.) |
| evaluate.py, evaluate_fanfic.py | Unchanged |
| write/loop.py | Unchanged (thin coordinator; orchestrator delegates) |
| Hermes plugin authoring | Primer at explore-7hh.17 closed; deep-dive at .19 (in flight) |

## 3. Changes and decisions

### 3.1 Phase 1 skill: autonovel-phase1-smoke
- **WHAT**: Hermes skill at `~/.hermes/skills/autonovel-phase1-smoke/SKILL.md` that wraps a single `delegate_task` call producing one paragraph of prose.
- **WHY**: simplest possible end-to-end demo. Validates skill discovery + delegate_task + writing to disk.
- **HOW**: SKILL.md body reads a hardcoded brief (post-Act-3 BG3 character study), constructs prompt via existing `write/prompts.py` (called via Python subprocess from delegate_task), writes output to `write/runs/phase1-smoke/draft.md`.

### 3.2 Phase 1 cron: autonovel-phase1-smoke
- **WHAT**: `hermes cron create '0 9 * * *' '/skill:autonovel-phase1-smoke run' --deliver telegram` (or local-file delivery)
- **WHY**: prove the daemon mode works; replaces Claude Code's heartbeat-driven invocation
- **HOW**: scheduled job fires daily; output bead-equivalent surface for review (file written + optional notification)

### 3.3 Phase 1 delegate target: prose generation
- **WHAT**: child Hermes agent dispatched via delegate_task with goal='Write one paragraph of BG3 fanfic continuing the supplied prompt'
- **WHY**: validates subagent pattern as replacement for Claude Code's Agent tool
- **HOW**: delegate_task config: restricted toolset (read, write only), model=custom/qwen3-coder:30b, iteration_budget=10

### 3.4 Reused autonovel code (unchanged)
- `write/prompts.py` — wrap_for_subagent, build_draft_system, build_draft_user
- `identity/self.md`, `voice_priors.json`, `few_shot_bank.md` — loaded as skill body context
- `evaluate.py` (slop_score core) — post-generation gate, surfaced as Pass/Fail in cron output

### 3.5 NOT building in Phase 1
- Custom Hermes plugins (deferred to .13-.15)
- Halt machinery beyond Hermes built-in iteration_budget
- AO3 client / staging / posting
- Voice-anchor injection via few-shot
- Multi-model routing (Phase 2 introduces tier'd models)

## 4. Formal specification

### 4.1 Skill body (~/.hermes/skills/autonovel-phase1-smoke/SKILL.md)

```yaml
---
name: autonovel-phase1-smoke
description: Phase 1 minimum-viable autonovel — produces one BG3 fanfic paragraph
---

# autonovel-phase1-smoke

Generate one paragraph of BG3 fanfic in autonovel's established voice. Phase 1 demo.

## What this skill does
1. Builds a brief (hardcoded scene: post-Act 3 Astarion/Karlach character study)
2. Loads voice (identity/self.md + voice_priors.json) as system context
3. Delegates to a child Hermes agent for prose generation
4. Saves to write/runs/phase1-smoke/draft-<timestamp>.md
5. Runs evaluate.py slop_score; report Pass/Fail
```

### 4.2 Cron registration

```bash
hermes cron create '0 9 * * *' '/skill:autonovel-phase1-smoke run' --deliver local-file:/home/ubuntu/explore/autonovel/feedback/phase1-cron.log
```

### 4.3 Delegate task definition

```python
# Inside skill body or via skill_view
result = delegate_task(
    goal="Write one paragraph (3-5 sentences) of BG3 fanfic continuing this scene: ...",
    tools=['read', 'write'],
    model='custom/qwen3-coder:30b',  # or 'qwen3-coder' if alias works through Hermes
    iteration_budget=10,
)
# result is the child agent's final output (the paragraph)
```

## 5. Tests / acceptance criteria

| Test | Pass criterion |
|---|---|
| T1: skill discovery | `hermes skills list` shows `autonovel-phase1-smoke` |
| T2: skill invocation | `hermes -z '/skill:autonovel-phase1-smoke run' --yolo` produces a paragraph |
| T3: file written | `write/runs/phase1-smoke/draft-<ts>.md` exists and contains prose |
| T4: cron registers | `hermes cron list` shows the job |
| T5: cron fires | next 09:00 fires, log file shows execution |
| T6: slop gate | evaluate.py slop_score < 5.0 |
| T7: voice match | manual blind-rate: ≥3/5 voice match to Entry 002 anchor |

## 6. Open questions

- **OQ-1**: do Hermes child agents inherit the parent's system prompt? If yes, identity context is automatic. If no, must pass explicitly via skill body. **Verify via .19 deep-dive empirical work.**
- **OQ-2**: does Hermes' `skill_view` wrapper poison voice files when they're loaded as skill context? (bd-b5p.2 OQ #2). Test by inspecting actual wire payload.
- **OQ-3**: does `WriteLoopState`-style state survive across cron firings or must it migrate to SessionDB? Phase 1 sidesteps by writing to disk; Phase 2 needs to decide.
- **OQ-4**: which cron delivery mechanism — local-file (simple) vs telegram (operationally useful for 'draft ready') vs slack vs both?
- **OQ-5**: does the cron prompt-injection scanner (primer §5) interfere with our skill body? Verify.
- **OQ-6**: Hermes' iteration_budget defaults (parent 90 / subagents 50) — sufficient for a one-paragraph generation? Probably overkill; just confirm doesn't trip.

## 7. Exit criteria for Phase 1

- All 7 tests pass
- Cron has fired ≥3 times unattended
- Slop scores within 0.5 of Claude-baseline calibration
- No Hermes upstream bug (D11) blocks the path

## 8. Hand-off to Phase 2

Phase 2 spec (bd-b5p.5, TBD) will build:
- Custom plugins (pico-tailnet provider, beads-observability)
- Multi-skill orchestration (/write /conceive /learn ported)
- Halt-machinery integration
- Voice-anchor injection via few_shot_bank
- AO3 staging integration

## Cross-references

- bd-b5p.2 (architecture research; the source of Phase 1 design)
- bd-b5p.1 (Epic D spec; original wrong-turn — historical)
- explore-7hh.17 (Hermes primer)
- explore-7hh.19 (plugin deep-dive, in flight; verifies our authoring path)
- ~/explore/local-coding-models/refs/research/research-autonovel-on-hermes.md
- D11 in dotfiles-5e2 (live Hermes upstream bugs that could affect Phase 1 cron path)
notes:
---

## OQ resolutions — 2026-05-26 (Wave 14C empirical)

Per subagent handoff (commit 6ef1c0a, cherry-picked as autonovel master a9f4338):

**Empirically answered:**
- **OQ-3** (state survives across cron firings?) — CONFIRMED via filesystem. One file per firing under UTC-timestamped name (`draft-<ISO>.md`). Phase 1 sidesteps SessionDB.
- **OQ-4** (delivery channel?) — DECIDED: `--deliver local`. No auth, simplest operational path. Operator reviews `~/.hermes/cron/output/autonovel-phase1-smoke/*.md`.
- **OQ-6** (iteration_budget defaults sufficient?) — CONFIRMED yes. Single-shot HTTP POST, no recursive tool use; well within parent-90 / subagent-50 defaults.

**Sidestepped (must answer in Phase 2):**
- **OQ-1** (Hermes child agents inherit parent system prompt?) — Phase 1 runner passes identity inline via `_PROSE_FRAME` wrap rather than relying on inheritance. Phase 2 migration to true `delegate_task` will need empirical answer.
- **OQ-2** (skill_view poisons voice files?) — Phase 1 runner reads `identity/*.md` directly, not via skill_view. Phase 2 (when `autonovel-voice` skill is introduced) needs to test.

**Pending operator observation:**
- **OQ-5** (prompt-injection scanner interference?) — Not triggered during the registered cron's brief existence (job removed before next 09:00 tick). Operator should watch first real firing for `CronPromptInjectionBlocked` in Hermes logs.

## Smoke results (Wave 14C empirical)

All 5 testable acceptance criteria PASS (T1-T4 + T6):
- T1 SKILL.md discoverable: PASS (`hermes skills list` shows autonovel-phase1-smoke)
- T2 runner produces prose: PASS (105s warm-path, 1189-byte draft)
- T3 draft-*.md file written: PASS
- T4 hermes cron list shows job: PASS (registered + verified, then removed)
- T5 next-09:00 firing: OPERATOR (documented)
- T6 slop_score < 5.0: PASS (slop_penalty=0.0 on Karlach POV sample)
- T7 human blind-rate: OPERATOR (documented)

Sample evidence draft: `write/runs/phase1-smoke/draft-20260526T044832Z.md`

## Cron-create gotcha discovered

`hermes cron create` requires `--name/--skill/--workdir/--deliver` BEFORE the positional `schedule` and `prompt` args. Otherwise argparse mis-routes the prompt as "unrecognized arguments". README has the corrected invocation.

## Phase 2 candidates (per sidestepped OQs + spec)

- Migrate runner to true `delegate_task` (answers OQ-1 + OQ-2)
- Voice-anchor injection via `few_shot_bank` (currently sidestepped)
- AO3 staging integration
- Multi-skill orchestration
- Halt-machinery beyond Hermes built-in iteration_budget
- Custom plugins (pico-tailnet via explore-7hh.13, beads-observability via explore-7hh.15)

title: spec: bd-b5p Phase 2 — delegate_task + voice anchors + AO3 staging
description:
# Spec: Phase 2 — delegate_task migration + voice anchors + AO3 staging

**Bead:** bd-b5p.5 · **Phase:** 2 of 4 · **Status:** spec
**Parent:** bd-b5p (epic) · **Predecessor:** bd-b5p.4 (Phase 1, closed)

## 1. Overview

Phase 1 (bd-b5p.4) proved autonovel composes end-to-end on Hermes via a deliberately minimum-viable HTTP shim (363-line runner.py POSTing directly to the OpenAI-compatible endpoint). It sidestepped two architectural OQs that can only be answered by running on Hermes-native primitives:

- **OQ-1**: do Hermes child agents inherit the parent's system prompt?
- **OQ-2**: does `skill_view` poison voice files when loaded as skill context?

Phase 2 makes the migration real along three orthogonal axes, each of which earns its own §4 sub-spec:

1. **delegate_task migration** — replace `runner.py`'s direct HTTP POST with a Hermes-native `delegate_task` call invoked by the parent agent during a conversation turn (the intended use-pattern; HTTP shim was Phase 1 expediency). This is the venue where OQ-1 and OQ-2 are answered empirically.
2. **few_shot_bank voice-anchor injection** — promote `identity/few_shot_bank.md` from "first 3K chars dumped into the prompt" (the Phase 1 punt) to a structured POV-character-aware injector that selects exemplar passages matching the brief's POV character, with a downstream "voice anchors passes" gate complementing slop_penalty.
3. **AO3 staging** — local-staged-but-not-posted integration: the draft → publish queue pipeline lands in `publish_queue/` per the existing `api/queue.py` shape with full publish metadata (rating, fandom, tags, warnings, summary). Actual AO3 API posting (the live POST to ao3.org) is deferred to Phase 3.

**Goal:** `hermes -z '/skill:autonovel-phase2 run' --yolo` produces one paragraph via `delegate_task`, with voice anchors selected by POV character, that lands in `publish_queue/<queue-id>.json` with status=PENDING — fully shaped for AO3 posting but never actually posted.

**Hypothesis (testable):** every Phase 1 sidestep has a Hermes-native answer that is at least as good as the shim. If `delegate_task` doesn't poison the `_PROSE_FRAME` contract, if `skill_view` doesn't wrap voice files with attribution noise, and if `publish_queue/` already accommodates a Hermes-driven enqueue without surgery, then Phase 2 is a substrate change with zero algorithmic regression.

**Rollback:** if Phase 2 regresses (slop_penalty creep, preamble leakage, voice-match drop), the Phase 1 runner.py stays operational and unchanged at `hermes-skills/autonovel-phase1-smoke/runner.py`. The Phase 2 skill is a NEW skill (`autonovel-phase2`), not a replacement of `autonovel-phase1-smoke`. Reverting = stop scheduling Phase 2's cron, resume Phase 1's. See §6 OQ-7.

## 2. Current state / baseline (operational facts at Phase 2 start)

| Component | Status (post-Phase 1) |
|---|---|
| Hermes Agent v0.14.0 | Installed; cron path verified |
| `autonovel-phase1-smoke` skill | Operational; 363-line runner.py uses direct HTTP POST |
| Phase 1 acceptance T1–T4 + T6 | PASS (105s warm-path, 1189-byte draft, slop_penalty=0.0 on Karlach POV sample) |
| `delegate_task` available | Yes (`tools/delegate_tool.py:1918`); not yet used by autonovel |
| `_build_child_system_prompt` | Prepends "You are a focused subagent..." + appends summary instructions (`:569-642`); this is the OQ-1 collision risk |
| `DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS` | `delegate_task, clarify, memory, send_message, execute_code` (`:45-53`) — children cannot recurse or message users |
| `delegation.max_iterations` | Config-authoritative; model-supplied value ignored (`:1976-1988`). Default 50 for children. |
| `_subagent_auto_deny` | Default (safe). For prose-only delegates that don't need destructive ops, this is fine. |
| `skill_view` | Returns `{success, content}`; `cron/scheduler.py:1145-1162` injects "The user has invoked the `{skill_name}` skill" preamble |
| `identity/few_shot_bank.md` | 5 entries (Karlach×2, Astarion, Shadowheart×2); all reader-validated or internal-eval-validated; explicitly tagged by POV |
| `write/prompts.py` | UNCHANGED — `_PROSE_FRAME`, `build_draft_system`, `build_draft_user`, `wrap_for_subagent` all production-ready |
| `evaluate.py::slop_score` | UNCHANGED — `slop_penalty` numeric returned |
| `write/evaluate_fanfic.py` | UNCHANGED — `evaluate_gate` returns `(passed, reason)`; SLOP=3.0, QUALITY=7.0, CHARACTERIZATION=6.0 |
| `api/queue.py::enqueue` | UNCHANGED — `enqueue(PublishRequest) -> QueueItem` writes to `publish_queue/<id>.json` |
| `api/models.py::PublishRequest` | UNCHANGED — `{title, fandom, rating, tags, summary, body, author_notes}` |
| AO3 actual POST path | Out of scope for Phase 2; `api/ao3_client.py` exists but not invoked from Phase 2 skill |

**What's sidestepped going INTO Phase 2 (from Phase 1's OQ-resolution addendum):**
- OQ-1 (child inherits parent system prompt?) — unanswered. Phase 1 used HTTP, so no child agent existed.
- OQ-2 (skill_view poisons voice files?) — unanswered. Phase 1 read identity files directly with `Path.read_text`.

**What's NEWLY-true going INTO Phase 2 (Phase 1 empirical results):**
- OQ-3 (state survives cron firings?) — CONFIRMED via filesystem. UTC-timestamped files; no SessionDB needed.
- OQ-4 (delivery channel?) — DECIDED: `--deliver local`. Operator reviews `~/.hermes/cron/output/`.
- OQ-6 (iteration_budget sufficient?) — CONFIRMED. Single-shot generation well within parent-90 / subagent-50 defaults.

## 3. Changes and decisions

### 3.1 New skill: `autonovel-phase2`
- **WHAT**: New skill at `hermes-skills/autonovel-phase2/SKILL.md` (NOT a replacement of `autonovel-phase1-smoke` — both coexist; Phase 1 stays as rollback target).
- **WHY**: clean substrate. Phase 1's runner.py stays untouched as a fallback. The new skill is the one we'd register a new cron against.
- **HOW**: SKILL.md body instructs the parent agent to call `delegate_task` with a goal constructed from the brief + identity + selected voice anchors. The parent runs the slop gate and the queue-enqueue itself (those are not delegated — they're pure Python the parent runs via terminal_tool / direct import).

### 3.2 delegate_task contract (resolves OQ-1, OQ-2)

- **WHAT**: parent agent calls `delegate_task(goal=<wrapped frame>, context="", toolsets=[], role="leaf", max_iterations=None)`.
- **WHY**: Hermes-native subagent path; isolated context; no Claude-Code persona drift; trajectory captured for Phase 4 voice training.
- **HOW (the resolution of OQ-1)**: pass the full `_PROSE_FRAME`-wrapped prompt as `goal`, leave `context` empty. The child's system prompt becomes Hermes' boilerplate ("You are a focused subagent...") PLUS our goal. The OQ-1 collision risk is whether Hermes' boilerplate fights `_PROSE_FRAME`'s OUTPUT RULES. The decision is to ACCEPT the boilerplate concatenation and rely on `_PROSE_FRAME`'s explicit "Do not break character. Do not refer to yourself, the task, or this prompt." line to override. We verify empirically via T-Delegate-3 below.
- **DECISION on toolsets**: `toolsets=[]` (no tools). The child is a pure generator — no `read`, no `write`, no `terminal_tool`. It receives the entire prompt as `goal` and emits prose as its final summary message. This is intentional: keeps the child's context narrow, eliminates tool-use latency, and matches the `_PROSE_FRAME` contract that the response IS the story.
- **DECISION on role**: `role="leaf"` (default). No nested delegation. Single-shot prose generation has no internal parallelism.
- **DECISION on model**: inherit parent's. The parent agent already runs on `custom/qwen3-coder:30b` (or whatever `~/.hermes/config.yaml` declares). Child inherits via Hermes' `_resolve_delegation_credentials` path. No per-task model override.
- **DECISION on max_iterations**: leave default (Hermes' `delegation.max_iterations` = 50). Phase 1 confirmed this is ample. The kwarg is ignored anyway (`delegate_tool.py:1976-1988`).
- **NEW OQ raised**: does Hermes' `_build_child_system_prompt` summary-format suffix ("provide a clear, concise summary of: What you did / What you found...") cause the model to emit preamble like "Here is the paragraph I generated:" before the prose? If so, `_PROSE_FRAME`'s "Do not refer to yourself" line should override, but verify in T-Delegate-3.

### 3.3 skill_view vs direct read (resolves OQ-2)

- **WHAT**: load `identity/self.md`, `identity/voice_priors.json`, `identity/few_shot_bank.md` via `Path.read_text` from the parent skill body — NOT via `skill_view`.
- **WHY**: per OQ-2, `skill_view` triggers `cron/scheduler.py:1145-1162` which prepends "The user has invoked the `{skill_name}` skill..." attribution noise. That preamble would contaminate the voice context the model sees. Direct read keeps identity raw.
- **DECISION**: defer `autonovel-voice` skill packaging (a separate skill bundling identity files) to Phase 3 or beyond. Phase 2 reads identity files directly the same way runner.py did. OQ-2 is RESOLVED as "skill_view contaminates → don't use it for voice."
- **CONSEQUENCE**: identity loading code from runner.py (`_load_identity_context`, `_read_text`) is extracted into a shared helper at `hermes-skills/autonovel-phase2/identity_loader.py` so Phase 2 skill body uses it AND Phase 1 runner.py can be refactored to import it (optional cleanup; not required for Phase 2 acceptance).

### 3.4 Voice-anchor selection (few_shot_bank.md → POV-aware)

- **WHAT**: replace runner.py's "first 3000 chars of few_shot_bank.md" punt with a structured selector that:
  1. Parses `identity/few_shot_bank.md` into entries (split on `^## Entry \d+`).
  2. Extracts each entry's POV (regex `^\*\*POV:\*\* (.+)$` per entry block).
  3. Selects entries whose POV matches the brief's POV character (case-insensitive substring match on the first name — "Karlach" matches "Karlach (with Dammon in dialogue)").
  4. Caps at MAX_ANCHORS = 2 selected anchors per draft (token budget; 3+ anchors push past qwen3-coder:30b's effective comfort zone for the rest of the prompt).
  5. Fallback: if zero POV matches, use the 2 most-recently-mined entries (by header date metadata).
- **WHY**: a Karlach POV draft should see Karlach exemplars, not Astarion's. Phase 1 dumped all 5 entries indiscriminately and hoped for the best.
- **HOW**: new helper at `hermes-skills/autonovel-phase2/anchor_selector.py` with one function `select_anchors(few_shot_text: str, pov_character: str, max_anchors: int = 2) -> list[str]` returning the raw markdown blocks (between `## Entry N` headers) for the matched entries.
- **PROMPT INJECTION**: selected anchors are concatenated and slotted into the existing `build_draft_system`'s `identity` block under a clearly-labeled "VOICE ANCHORS (recent successful samples — match this register):" header (same shape as runner.py's slot, just with POV-aware content).

### 3.5 Voice-anchors-passes gate (complementing slop_penalty)

- **WHAT**: post-generation, in addition to `slop_score`, compute a "voice anchor match" score that measures whether the draft prose echoes structural devices from the selected anchors.
- **WHY**: slop_penalty catches AI tells (banned words, structural tics). It does NOT catch "the prose is fine but doesn't sound like ME." The few_shot_bank entries each enumerate specific "devices demonstrated" (e.g. "structural list with final pivot", "collaborative metaphor-building through dialogue") — Phase 2 uses these as a soft positive signal.
- **HOW (Phase 2 minimal version)**: NOT a model-based evaluator (too expensive for Phase 2; that's Phase 3+). Instead, a heuristic checker at `hermes-skills/autonovel-phase2/voice_match.py`:
  - Computes prose length, em-dash count per 1000 words, dialogue-tag-to-narration ratio, average sentence length variance — the same dimensions the anchors collectively demonstrate.
  - Compares against the corresponding stats computed from the SELECTED anchor passages.
  - Returns `voice_match_score` ∈ [0.0, 1.0] where 1.0 = stats within 1σ of anchor stats, 0.0 = stats >3σ off.
- **GATE**: `voice_match_score >= 0.5` is PASS. Below that, log a warning but DO NOT block the draft from queuing (Phase 2 surfaces the signal; Phase 3 may promote it to a hard gate). Rationale: voice-match is a soft signal; we want operator-in-the-loop calibration before making it block-grade.

### 3.6 AO3 staging (publish_queue/, not the live POST)

- **WHAT**: after slop gate passes, the parent agent constructs a `PublishRequest` from the generated prose + brief metadata and calls `api.queue.enqueue(request)` to land it in `publish_queue/<queue-id>.json` with `status=PENDING`.
- **WHY**: the existing `api/queue.py` is already the AO3 staging seam — `publish_queue/` is the human-review hand-off point before the operator approves a live POST to ao3.org. Phase 2 just connects autonovel's Hermes output to that existing seam.
- **HOW**: parent skill body imports `api.queue.enqueue` and `api.models.PublishRequest` directly (parent is Python; no need to terminal_tool out to it). Construction:
  - `title`: derived from brief — Phase 2 uses a hardcoded title per brief (e.g. "Garden Bench" for the Phase 1 hardcoded brief). Phase 3 reads from `briefs/<slug>.json`.
  - `fandom`: `"Baldur's Gate 3 (Video Game)"` (AO3 canonical tag form). Hardcoded for Phase 2; Phase 3 reads from `briefs/*.json`.
  - `rating`: `Rating.GENERAL` for Phase 2's character-study brief.
  - `tags`: hardcoded `["Karlach (Baldur's Gate)", "Astarion (Baldur's Gate)", "Post-Canon", "Character Study", "Hurt/Comfort"]` for the Phase 2 hardcoded brief; brief-driven in Phase 3.
  - `summary`: first sentence of generated prose, truncated to 250 chars (AO3 summary limit).
  - `body`: the generated prose verbatim (no footer, no metadata — those go in `author_notes`).
  - `author_notes`: a `<!-- autonovel metadata ... -->` HTML comment with bead ID, model, base_url, slop_penalty, voice_match_score, timestamp. Hidden from AO3 readers but human-auditable.
- **OUT OF SCOPE**: actual `httpx.post` to AO3. The QueueItem sits at `PENDING` indefinitely; operator manually promotes to `PUBLISHED` via existing `api/queue.py` machinery (Phase 3+ work).
- **STAGING INTEGRATION TEST**: `hermes-skills/autonovel-phase2/staging-test.sh` runs the full Phase 2 path end-to-end against a temp `publish_queue/` directory (env override) and asserts a valid QueueItem JSON lands with all expected fields.

### 3.7 Cron registration (Phase 2)
- **WHAT**: NEW cron job `autonovel-phase2` registered alongside (NOT replacing) `autonovel-phase1-smoke`. Same `--deliver local`, same 09:00 schedule, different `--name` and `--workdir`.
- **WHY**: parallel operation lets the operator A/B Phase 1 vs Phase 2 outputs side-by-side for one observation week before retiring Phase 1's cron.
- **HOW**: `hermes cron create --name autonovel-phase2 --skill autonovel-phase2 --workdir /home/ubuntu/explore/autonovel --deliver local '0 9 * * *' 'Run the autonovel-phase2 skill: generate one paragraph and stage it for AO3 review.'`

### 3.8 NOT building in Phase 2 (explicit non-goals)
- Reader feedback loop (`/feedback`, `/mail`) — Phase 3
- Actual AO3 POST (`httpx.post` against ao3.org) — Phase 3
- Multi-skill orchestration (`/heartbeat`, `/daily`, `/conceive`, `/learn`) — Phase 3
- Halt machinery beyond Hermes built-in `iteration_budget` — not needed; Phase 1 confirmed defaults sufficient
- Brief reading from `briefs/<slug>.json` — Phase 3 (Phase 2 hardcodes the same brief Phase 1 used)
- `autonovel-voice` skill packaging via `skill_view` — explicitly REJECTED in Phase 2 per OQ-2 resolution; revisit only if `skill_view` adds a `--raw` mode upstream
- Model-based voice evaluator — Phase 3+ (Phase 2 uses heuristic stats)

## 4. Formal specification

### 4.1 Skill body (`hermes-skills/autonovel-phase2/SKILL.md`)

```yaml
---
name: autonovel-phase2
description: "Phase 2 autonovel — Hermes-native delegate_task generation with POV-aware voice anchors, staged to publish_queue/ for AO3 review (no live posting). Bead bd-b5p.5."
version: "0.2.0"
author: "autonovel"
license: MIT
platforms: [linux, macos]
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [autonovel, fanfic, phase2, delegate-task, voice-anchors, ao3-staging, bg3]
    related_skills: [autonovel-phase1-smoke]
    category: creative
---

# autonovel-phase2

## What this skill does
1. Builds a brief (hardcoded same as Phase 1 for direct comparability).
2. Selects POV-appropriate voice anchors from identity/few_shot_bank.md.
3. Calls delegate_task(goal=_PROSE_FRAME-wrapped prompt, context="", role="leaf", toolsets=[]).
4. Runs evaluate.py slop_score on the child's response.
5. Runs voice_match.py heuristic score against selected anchors.
6. Constructs api.models.PublishRequest from prose + brief metadata.
7. Calls api.queue.enqueue to stage at publish_queue/<id>.json (status=PENDING).
8. Returns single-line summary: slop_penalty / voice_match_score / queue_id / status.
```

### 4.2 delegate_task call (the OQ-1/OQ-2 resolution venue)

```python
from write.prompts import build_draft_system, build_draft_user, wrap_for_subagent

system_p = build_draft_system(context_dict, soul_text)  # context_dict from identity_loader
user_p = build_draft_user(brief, context_dict, chapter_num=1, total_chapters=1, ...)
prompt = wrap_for_subagent(system_p, user_p)  # _PROSE_FRAME envelope

# Hermes-native delegate (replaces Phase 1 HTTP POST)
result_json = delegate_task(
    goal=prompt,         # entire frame as goal
    context="",          # empty — frame is self-contained
    toolsets=[],         # pure generator, no tools
    role="leaf",         # no nested delegation
    max_iterations=None, # use config default (50)
)
result = json.loads(result_json)
prose = result["results"][0]["summary"]  # child's final-message text
```

### 4.3 Voice-anchor selector (anchor_selector.py)

```python
def select_anchors(
    few_shot_text: str,
    pov_character: str,
    max_anchors: int = 2,
) -> list[str]:
    """Parse few_shot_bank.md entries; return up to max_anchors raw entry blocks
    whose POV line case-insensitively contains pov_character's first name.
    Fallback: if zero matches, return the max_anchors most-recently-dated entries
    (by their **Source:** date suffix).
    """
```

Entry parsing: split text on `\n## Entry \d+\n` (re.split with capture); each entry block runs from its header to the next entry or EOF. POV extraction: per-entry regex `^\*\*POV:\*\* (.+)$` (multiline). Date extraction: per-entry regex on **Source:** line.

### 4.4 Voice-match heuristic (voice_match.py)

```python
def voice_match_score(prose: str, anchor_passages: list[str]) -> float:
    """Compute four normalized metrics on prose AND on each anchor passage:
    - avg_sentence_length
    - sentence_length_std (variance proxy)
    - em_dashes_per_1000_words
    - dialogue_ratio (proportion of sentences with '"...,"' or '"...?"' or '"...!"' shape)

    Pool anchor stats (mean + std across anchors). For each metric, compute
    z-score = abs(prose_value - anchor_mean) / max(anchor_std, epsilon).
    Per-metric score = 1.0 if z<=1, 0.5 if z<=3, 0.0 otherwise.
    Final score = mean of the four per-metric scores. Range [0.0, 1.0].
    Gate: PASS if >= 0.5 (advisory only in Phase 2).
    """
```

### 4.5 PublishRequest construction (parent skill body)

```python
from api.models import PublishRequest, Rating
from api.queue import enqueue

summary = prose.split(".")[0].strip()[:250]
req = PublishRequest(
    title="Garden Bench",  # hardcoded for Phase 2
    fandom="Baldur's Gate 3 (Video Game)",
    rating=Rating.GENERAL,
    tags=[
        "Karlach (Baldur's Gate)",
        "Astarion (Baldur's Gate)",
        "Post-Canon",
        "Character Study",
        "Hurt/Comfort",
    ],
    summary=summary,
    body=prose,
    author_notes=f"<!-- autonovel-phase2 metadata\nbead: bd-b5p.5\nmodel: {model}\nslop_penalty: {slop_penalty}\nvoice_match_score: {vm_score}\nts: {ts_utc}\n-->",
)
item = enqueue(req)  # writes publish_queue/<item.queue_id>.json
```

### 4.6 Single-line summary (stdout, for `--deliver local`)

```
slop_penalty=<float> voice_match=<float> queue_id=<hex12> status=<PASS|FAIL>
```

PASS iff `slop_penalty < 3.0` (production threshold, NOT Phase 1's relaxed 5.0). FAIL = staging is skipped (queue entry NOT created).

### 4.7 Cron job spec

```bash
hermes cron create \
  --name autonovel-phase2 \
  --skill autonovel-phase2 \
  --workdir /home/ubuntu/explore/autonovel \
  --deliver local \
  '0 9 * * *' \
  'Run the autonovel-phase2 skill: generate one paragraph and stage it for AO3 review.'
```

Coexists with `autonovel-phase1-smoke` for one observation week, then Phase 1 cron is removed (skill files stay as rollback artifact).

## 5. Test cases

Each test has INPUT / EXPECTED / RATIONALE. Tests are organized by sub-system: D=delegate_task, V=voice anchors, A=AO3 staging, E=end-to-end.

### T-D-1: delegate_task is invoked (no HTTP fallback)

- **INPUT**: run skill; capture network calls and Hermes audit log.
- **EXPECTED**: Hermes audit log shows `delegate_task` invocation with `role=leaf`, `toolsets=[]`. No outbound HTTP from skill code (only Hermes' internal child-agent HTTP to the provider, which is normal).
- **RATIONALE**: confirms the migration off the Phase 1 HTTP shim is complete — the skill is not silently still POSTing direct.

### T-D-2: child receives the wrapped prompt as `goal`

- **INPUT**: instrument `_build_child_system_prompt` (or read Hermes session DB for the child's actual system prompt); run the skill.
- **EXPECTED**: child's effective system prompt contains the verbatim `_PROSE_FRAME` text ("You will execute one task only: generate prose..."). Hermes' boilerplate "You are a focused subagent..." prepends but does not replace.
- **RATIONALE**: OQ-1 resolution evidence. Confirms the persona-suppression contract survives delegate_task wrapping.

### T-D-3: no preamble leakage in child response

- **INPUT**: run skill; inspect raw `prose` returned by delegate_task BEFORE any post-processing.
- **EXPECTED**: first 100 chars of prose contain NO "Here is", "Here's", "I'll", "Sure,", "The following", "Below is", or any of the OUTPUT_RULES violations from `_PROSE_FRAME`. The first character is a quote, a capital letter starting a sentence, or another prose-opening character.
- **RATIONALE**: confirms `_PROSE_FRAME` overrides Hermes' "provide a clear, concise summary of: What you did..." suffix in `_build_child_system_prompt`. If this fails, the wrap_for_subagent contract is broken in Hermes substrate and Phase 2 cannot ship.

### T-D-4: identity files loaded via direct read, NOT skill_view

- **INPUT**: trace which Hermes tools the parent uses while loading identity. (Static review of skill body suffices.)
- **EXPECTED**: parent skill body uses Python `Path.read_text` (via `identity_loader.py`) for `self.md`, `voice_priors.json`, `few_shot_bank.md`. Zero `skill_view` calls for identity content.
- **RATIONALE**: OQ-2 resolution. Avoids `cron/scheduler.py:1145-1162` attribution noise contaminating the voice context.

### T-V-1: POV selector returns Karlach anchors for Karlach brief

- **INPUT**: `select_anchors(few_shot_bank_text, "Karlach", max_anchors=2)`.
- **EXPECTED**: returned list has length 2; both entries' POV lines case-insensitively contain "Karlach". With the current bank, this returns Entry 001 (Karlach) and Entry 005 (Karlach with Dammon).
- **RATIONALE**: per-POV exemplar selection is the whole reason for §3.4.

### T-V-2: POV selector fallback when no matches

- **INPUT**: `select_anchors(few_shot_bank_text, "Halsin", max_anchors=2)` (Halsin not in current bank).
- **EXPECTED**: returned list has length 2; entries are the two most-recently-dated by **Source:** date suffix.
- **RATIONALE**: fallback path is exercised; selector never returns empty when bank is non-empty.

### T-V-3: voice-match score for prose echoing anchor stats

- **INPUT**: prose string assembled by sampling sentences from the selected anchors directly.
- **EXPECTED**: `voice_match_score >= 0.8` (essentially identical stat distributions).
- **RATIONALE**: sanity check — the heuristic recognizes its own anchor stats. If this fails, the heuristic is broken.

### T-V-4: voice-match score for adversarial off-voice prose

- **INPUT**: prose like "It was a dark and stormy night. There were many things. They happened. The end." (uniformly short, no dialogue, no em-dashes — opposite shape from anchors).
- **EXPECTED**: `voice_match_score <= 0.3`.
- **RATIONALE**: heuristic discriminates off-voice prose. Confirms it's not stuck-at-pass.

### T-V-5: voice-match warning, not block, on Phase 2

- **INPUT**: run skill on a brief where the generated prose scores `voice_match=0.2` but `slop_penalty=1.0`.
- **EXPECTED**: queue item IS still enqueued (Phase 2 doesn't block on voice-match); single-line summary includes `voice_match=0.2`; logged warning visible in `~/.hermes/cron/output/`.
- **RATIONALE**: §3.5 decision — voice-match is advisory in Phase 2. If the gate accidentally blocks, the Phase 2 contract is broken.

### T-A-1: PublishRequest fields all set

- **INPUT**: run end-to-end with PASS-grade prose.
- **EXPECTED**: `publish_queue/<id>.json` contains `publish_request` with all 7 fields populated (title, fandom, rating, tags non-empty, summary non-empty, body non-empty, author_notes non-empty). `status` == `"pending"`. `ao3_work_id` is null.
- **RATIONALE**: confirms the queue contract is fully shaped, not a stub.

### T-A-2: summary truncation respects 250-char limit

- **INPUT**: prose whose first sentence exceeds 250 chars.
- **EXPECTED**: `publish_request.summary` length ≤ 250.
- **RATIONALE**: AO3 enforces 250-char summary; sending oversized would error at live-POST time.

### T-A-3: author_notes contains bead ID + slop + voice scores

- **INPUT**: any successful staging run.
- **EXPECTED**: `publish_request.author_notes` contains substring `bd-b5p.5`, the numeric slop_penalty, and the numeric voice_match_score, wrapped in HTML comment so it doesn't render on AO3.
- **RATIONALE**: human auditability — the operator reviewing the queue can see which model/params produced this.

### T-A-4: slop gate failure skips queue entry

- **INPUT**: prose with `slop_penalty=4.5` (above the 3.0 production threshold).
- **EXPECTED**: NO new file in `publish_queue/`; stdout summary says `status=FAIL`; exit code 1.
- **RATIONALE**: failing prose must not pollute the human-review queue. The gate is the firewall.

### T-A-5: no live AO3 POST attempted

- **INPUT**: run end-to-end with PASS-grade prose; inspect outbound network and `api/ao3_client.py` import telemetry.
- **EXPECTED**: zero `httpx.post` calls to any `archiveofourown.org` URL. `api.ao3_client` module is NOT imported by the Phase 2 skill path.
- **RATIONALE**: §3.8 non-goal. Phase 2 stages; Phase 3 posts.

### T-E-1: end-to-end smoke (the canonical Phase 2 acceptance run)

- **INPUT**: `hermes -z '/skill:autonovel-phase2 run' --yolo` (or equivalent invocation per Hermes 0.14 CLI).
- **EXPECTED**: stdout single-line summary; `publish_queue/<id>.json` exists with PENDING status; slop_penalty < 3.0; exit code 0; total wall-clock < 180s (warm-path; cold first-call up to 5 min acceptable).
- **RATIONALE**: the integration test that says Phase 2 ships.

### T-E-2: Phase 2 + Phase 1 coexistence

- **INPUT**: `hermes cron list` after both crons registered; trigger both manually (`hermes cron run autonovel-phase1-smoke && hermes cron run autonovel-phase2`).
- **EXPECTED**: both produce their respective outputs without colliding. Phase 1 writes to `write/runs/phase1-smoke/`; Phase 2 writes to `publish_queue/`. Disjoint output paths.
- **RATIONALE**: §3.1 decision — Phase 1 remains as rollback. Confirms no shared mutable state.

### T-E-3: rollback path is one-command

- **INPUT**: simulate Phase 2 regression: `hermes cron pause autonovel-phase2 && hermes cron resume autonovel-phase1-smoke`.
- **EXPECTED**: Phase 2 cron stops firing; Phase 1 continues unchanged. No data loss, no migration of in-flight queue items.
- **RATIONALE**: OQ-7 — rollback must be operationally trivial. Skill code coexists; no file deletion needed to roll back.

## 6. Open questions

### OQ-1 (FROM PHASE 1) — does the child inherit the parent's system prompt?

- **STATUS**: addressed empirically by T-D-2. Decision: NO inheritance; child's system prompt is Hermes-built ("You are a focused subagent...") + `goal`. We compensate by passing the full `_PROSE_FRAME`-wrapped prompt as `goal`. The empirical evidence from T-D-2/T-D-3 either ratifies this approach or forces a redesign.
- **CONTINGENCY**: if T-D-3 shows preamble leakage, options are (a) prepend an even-stronger anti-preamble preface to the `goal` string, (b) fork `_build_child_system_prompt` via Hermes plugin to skip the boilerplate, or (c) revert to Phase 1's HTTP path for prose-generation only while Hermes upstream is engaged on the issue.

### OQ-2 (FROM PHASE 1) — does skill_view poison voice files?

- **STATUS**: addressed by §3.3 design decision (don't use skill_view for identity). Decision: YES, skill_view prepends attribution noise (`cron/scheduler.py:1145-1162`); voice files are loaded via direct `Path.read_text` instead.
- **CONTINGENCY**: if Hermes upstream adds a `skill_view(name, raw=True)` mode, revisit and package `autonovel-voice` as its own skill (originally Phase 3 plan from bd-b5p.2). Until then, direct read is the path.

### OQ-3 — Hermes boilerplate "provide a summary" interferes with prose output?

- **STATUS**: NEW in Phase 2. The `_build_child_system_prompt` suffix tells the child to "provide a clear, concise summary of: What you did / What you found...". This is fundamentally at odds with `_PROSE_FRAME`'s "output ONLY the story prose." The prose IS the summary, but the model may add a meta-line ("Here is the paragraph:") to satisfy both contracts.
- **DECISION VENUE**: T-D-3. If it passes, OQ-3 resolves "frame overrides boilerplate." If it fails, the contingency from OQ-1 applies.

### OQ-4 — voice-match threshold calibration

- **QUESTION**: 0.5 is the soft-gate threshold in §3.5. Is that right calibration? Phase 2 doesn't block on it, so a wrong threshold is non-fatal, but the operator needs a meaningful signal.
- **DECISION VENUE**: collect 7 days of `voice_match_score` values from cron runs; if scores cluster above 0.7, raise threshold; if below 0.4, lower (or revisit the heuristic). Recalibrate before Phase 3 promotes voice-match to hard-gate status.

### OQ-5 — should voice_priors.json be a third-party input to anchor_selector?

- **QUESTION**: `identity/voice_priors.json` contains structured voice constraints (forbidden registers, preferred sentence shapes). Currently the anchor_selector ignores it and only looks at few_shot_bank entries. Should the selector cross-reference voice_priors to weight anchors?
- **DECISION VENUE**: punt to Phase 3. Phase 2's anchor_selector is POV-only; voice_priors stays as system-prompt content via `build_draft_system`. Revisit if Phase 3 voice-match work shows POV-only selection is inadequate.

### OQ-6 — brief hardcoding hand-off to brief-file reading

- **QUESTION**: Phase 2 hardcodes the same brief Phase 1 used. When does brief reading from `briefs/<slug>.json` land — Phase 3 or carved out into a Phase 2.5?
- **DECISION VENUE**: Phase 3 spec writing. If brief-reading is small enough to slot into Phase 2 without scope creep, fold it; otherwise it's the first Phase 3 deliverable.

### OQ-7 — rollback semantics for in-flight queue items

- **QUESTION**: if Phase 2 is rolled back mid-week (T-E-3), what happens to Phase 2's already-enqueued items in `publish_queue/`?
- **DECISION**: they stay. `publish_queue/` is human-review queue; operator can inspect Phase 2 entries' author_notes (which include `bd-b5p.5`) and decide per-item to delete, retain for manual posting, or carry forward to a future Phase 2 revision.

### OQ-8 — should the parent agent run evaluate.py as a sandboxed terminal_tool call or as in-process import?

- **QUESTION**: §4.5 implies in-process import. But in-process import shares Python state with Hermes (sys.path mutation, module cache). Terminal subprocess is cleaner isolation but slower (~50ms overhead).
- **DECISION**: in-process import for Phase 2. The parent agent IS Python; evaluate.py is pure (no side effects). Phase 1's runner.py already does this without issue. Re-evaluate if Hermes plugin isolation becomes a concern.

### OQ-9 — telemetry: does Hermes' SessionDB capture the delegate child's trajectory?

- **QUESTION**: bd-b5p.2 §2 highlights `agent/trajectory.py` ShareGPT export as a Phase 4 voice-training enabler. Does the delegate_task path automatically populate this for the child agent, or do we need a hook?
- **DECISION VENUE**: out of scope for Phase 2 acceptance. Verify empirically during Phase 2 operation and document; if absent, file a Hermes upstream feature request OR build the hook in Phase 3.

### OQ-10 — handling the prompt-injection scanner on Phase 2's cron

- **QUESTION**: Phase 1 OQ-5 noted `CronPromptInjectionBlocked` could fire on the assembled cron prompt. Phase 2's brief is creative fiction with words like "Astarion" / "Karlach" / dialogue marks. Does the scanner flag any of these as injection candidates?
- **DECISION VENUE**: monitor first 7 days of Phase 2 cron firings in `~/.hermes/cron/output/` for any `CronPromptInjectionBlocked` events. If triggered, file a Hermes config/allowlist change. (Same monitoring posture Phase 1 documented but never triggered.)

## 7. Future considerations (Phase 3+ signals captured here)

- **Phase 3 candidates** (per bd-b5p.2 + Phase 2 OQs):
  - Reader feedback loop: `/feedback`, `/mail` skills on Hermes
  - Brief reading from `briefs/<slug>.json` (OQ-6)
  - Live AO3 POST via `api/ao3_client.py` invoked from a promotion skill
  - Multi-skill orchestration (`/heartbeat` as Hermes cron router)
  - `autonovel-voice` skill packaging (gated on Hermes' `skill_view --raw` capability, OQ-2 contingency)
  - voice-match promotion from advisory to hard-gate (OQ-4 calibration)
  - voice_priors.json cross-reference in anchor selection (OQ-5)
  - Trajectory hook for ShareGPT capture (OQ-9)
  - SessionDB FTS5 search ("find the run where I tried 2nd-person POV") — bd-b5p.2 §2

- **Phase 4 candidates** (voice-tuning loop, per bd-b5p.2 §8):
  - Collect successful trajectories via OQ-9 enabler
  - LoRA-tune Qwen3 / Hermes-3 on voice samples
  - A/B base vs fine-tune via blind-rate panels
  - Replace base model in `--model` config OR keep base + use fine-tune for revision passes

- **Architectural cleanup** (no specific phase):
  - Retire `autonovel-phase1-smoke` skill once Phase 2 has 4+ unattended-week stability
  - Extract `identity_loader.py` as a shared helper in `autonovel/identity/` (vs the Phase 2 skill directory) once a second skill needs it
  - Consider promoting `anchor_selector.py` + `voice_match.py` to `autonovel/identity/` as identity-tier helpers (Phase 3 if a second skill consumes them)

## Decision: OQ-1 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT (with one MODIFY)

**Question (verbatim):** does the child inherit the parent's system prompt?

**Author's Recommendation:** NO inheritance; child's system prompt is Hermes-built ("You are a focused subagent...") + `goal`. Pass the full `_PROSE_FRAME`-wrapped prompt as `goal`. Verify empirically via T-D-2.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** `delegate_tool.py:569-642` (`_build_child_system_prompt`) is the authoritative path. Per the spec's own §2 baseline table, this function prepends "You are a focused subagent..." and appends a summary-instructions suffix. The parent's own system prompt does NOT flow into the child — only the parent-supplied `goal` and `context` strings do. This is consistent with how Anthropic's Agent tool, Claude Code's `Task` tool, and OpenAI Swarm-style handoffs all work: child agents get a fresh persona instantiated from the parent's call args, not the parent's chat history.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) ACCEPT the boilerplate concatenation** (author's choice). Relies on `_PROSE_FRAME`'s "Do not break character. Do not refer to yourself, the task, or this prompt." line to override Hermes' framing. Pro: zero substrate fork; works with stock Hermes 0.14. Con: relies on prompt-discipline (qwen3-coder:30b's instruction-following on competing system-prompt clauses is good but not perfect).
  - **(B) Fork `_build_child_system_prompt` via Hermes plugin** to skip the boilerplate. Pro: cleanest separation between Hermes' agent contract and our prose contract. Con: introduces a substrate fork; ties Phase 2 to a plugin authoring task that isn't the spec's stated scope (the §1.x plugin work is on the explore-7hh epic, not bd-b5p).
  - **(C) Stay on HTTP shim** (Phase 1 path) for prose-generation indefinitely. Pro: zero risk of substrate fights. Con: defeats the entire Phase 2 thesis; the point of bd-b5p.5 is to prove Hermes-native composition.
- **Prior art:** Claude Code's subagent path (Agent tool) also prepends a boilerplate persona ("You are a specialist subagent...") and gets identical-shape prose out of `_PROSE_FRAME`-wrapped goals — this is the exact pattern Phase 1 was modeled against pre-Hermes. The risk profile is well-understood: the failure mode is preamble leakage (covered by OQ-3/T-D-3), not silent persona drift.
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT with one MODIFY** — proceed with option (A), but the T-D-2 acceptance criterion needs to be strengthened: it currently reads "child's effective system prompt contains the verbatim `_PROSE_FRAME` text." Add: "AND the `_build_child_system_prompt` boilerplate prefix is captured for the Phase 2 retrospective so we know exactly what we're competing with." Mechanism: dump the child's effective system prompt to `~/.hermes/cron/output/autonovel-phase2/<ts>-child-syspr.txt` once on first run, then drop the dump on subsequent runs (it's stable per Hermes version).

**Settlement: ACCEPT** (option A) with the T-D-2 strengthening above.

**Spec impact:** §5 T-D-2 EXPECTED line — add the boilerplate-dump capture as part of the test artifact. Acceptance Criteria item for T-D-2 unchanged.

**Contingency reaffirmed:** if T-D-3 fails (preamble leakage), the spec's stated fallbacks (stronger anti-preamble preface, `_build_child_system_prompt` plugin fork, or HTTP-fallback for prose only) remain valid — in that priority order.

---

## Decision: OQ-2 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT

**Question (verbatim):** does skill_view poison voice files?

**Author's Recommendation:** YES, skill_view prepends attribution noise (`cron/scheduler.py:1145-1162`); voice files are loaded via direct `Path.read_text` instead.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** The spec cites `cron/scheduler.py:1145-1162` as the injection site for the "The user has invoked the `{skill_name}` skill" preamble. Phase 1's runner.py already proves the workaround works (it uses `Path.read_text` for identity/* and produces voice-match-grade prose at slop_penalty=0.0 per the spec's §2 baseline table). The author's design decision §3.3 codifies this as the Phase 2 pattern via `identity_loader.py` (shared helper extracted from runner.py).
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Direct `Path.read_text` via `identity_loader.py`** (author's choice). Pro: known-working (Phase 1 evidence); preserves voice fidelity; one-time helper extraction is a net cleanup. Con: identity content lives in autonovel repo paths rather than in Hermes' skill-discovery layer, so the Phase 2 skill body has a hard-coded `autonovel_root` (already true for the Phase 1 runner).
  - **(B) Package `autonovel-voice` as its own Hermes skill** and load via `skill_view`. Pro: portable; could be reused by future skills; aligns with Hermes' skill-as-bundle model. Con: BLOCKED by the attribution-noise contamination — `skill_view` has no `--raw` mode in 0.14. Author explicitly defers this to "Phase 3 or beyond, gated on Hermes upstream `skill_view(raw=True)`."
  - **(C) Monkey-patch `skill_view` from the Phase 2 skill** to strip the attribution prefix. Pro: minimal change. Con: deeply unsafe; depends on Hermes internals; would break on any Hermes patch release. Reject.
- **Prior art:** the same anti-pattern (skill loaders that prepend opinionated framing) is a known sharp edge in Claude Code skills too — the workaround there is also "skip the framework, read the file directly." This is not a Hermes-specific oddity.
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT** — proceed with option (A). The Phase 2 skill body uses `identity_loader.py` (read directly). Defer `autonovel-voice` skill packaging until Hermes ships a `--raw` mode (a Hermes upstream FR worth filing under bd-b5p.2's "Phase 3 candidates"; not Phase 2 scope).

**Settlement: ACCEPT.**

**Spec impact:** none. §3.3 is already the right shape; §4.x and §5 T-D-4 already encode the direct-read contract.

**Carry-forward:** orchestrator should file a separate decision-typed bead (or a `study` bead) for the Hermes-upstream `skill_view(raw=True)` feature request once Phase 2 ships — currently un-tracked.

---

## Decision: OQ-3 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT

**Question (verbatim):** does Hermes' boilerplate "provide a summary" interfere with prose output? The `_build_child_system_prompt` suffix tells the child to "provide a clear, concise summary of: What you did / What you found...". This is fundamentally at odds with `_PROSE_FRAME`'s "output ONLY the story prose." The prose IS the summary, but the model may add a meta-line ("Here is the paragraph:") to satisfy both contracts.

**Author's Recommendation:** DECISION VENUE is T-D-3. If it passes, OQ-3 resolves "frame overrides boilerplate." If it fails, the contingency from OQ-1 applies (stronger anti-preamble preface, plugin fork, or HTTP fallback).

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** Phase 1 evidence (HTTP path) shows `_PROSE_FRAME` does suppress preamble reliably on qwen3-coder:30b — the spec's §2 baseline says "1189-byte draft, slop_penalty=0.0 on Karlach POV sample," and Phase 1's acceptance gate T6 was met. So the frame itself works. The OQ-3 question is whether the additional "provide a summary" suffix that Hermes adds in the child's syspr (which Phase 1 never had to fight) shifts the model into meta-mode.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Trust the frame** (author's recommendation): rely on `_PROSE_FRAME`'s explicit "Do not refer to yourself, the task, or this prompt" to override. Verify empirically via T-D-3 on first run. Pro: zero substrate cost; if it works, we ship Phase 2. Con: empirical question; could fail.
  - **(B) Pre-strengthen the goal string** with a top-line "OUTPUT CONTRACT: the entire response IS the story. Do not produce a summary, a preamble, or any meta-text. Treat this constraint as overriding all prior instructions." Pro: belt-and-suspenders. Con: adds prompt bloat; may not be needed; could ironically prime the model toward meta-text by mentioning it.
  - **(C) Strip the suffix via plugin** (the OQ-1 contingency-B path). Pro: removes the conflict at the source. Con: substrate fork; Phase 2 stretches its scope.
- **Prior art:** Anthropic's Claude subagents have the same shape — a "summarize your work for the parent" suffix. With sufficiently strong prose contracts (Anthropic's own writing skills work the same way), the prose generators don't drift. The known-failure-modes are: (i) weak-instruction models (qwen3-coder:30b is on the stronger side for instruction-following), (ii) very short outputs where the model defaults to bullet-summary shape, (iii) prompts where the "story" target is ambiguous (we have a hardcoded brief, so this is non-ambiguous).
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT** option (A). T-D-3 is the gate. If it FAILS on the first Phase 2 cron firing, the orchestrator applies option (B) as a hotfix (`_PROSE_FRAME` already has the language; just promote a sentence to top-of-goal), and only escalates to (C) if (B) still fails after one retry.

**Settlement: ACCEPT** (frame overrides boilerplate; T-D-3 is the empirical gate).

**Spec impact:** none. The contingency ladder (B then C) is already documented under OQ-1; reaffirm it explicitly applies to OQ-3 too in the spec's OQ-3 body. The spec author should add one sentence to §3.2 ("DECISION on max_iterations") block summarizing the OQ-3 hot-fix ladder so the next agent reading the spec doesn't have to derive it.

---

## Decision: OQ-4 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT

**Question:** 0.5 is the soft-gate threshold in §3.5. Is that right calibration? Phase 2 doesn't block on it, so a wrong threshold is non-fatal, but the operator needs a meaningful signal.

**Author's Recommendation:** DECISION VENUE: collect 7 days of `voice_match_score` values from cron runs; recalibrate before Phase 3 promotes voice-match to hard-gate status.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** The voice_match heuristic (§4.4) computes four normalized metrics (avg_sentence_length, sentence_length_std, em_dashes_per_1000_words, dialogue_ratio) and z-scores them against pooled anchor stats. Per-metric score is 1.0 if z≤1, 0.5 if z≤3, 0.0 otherwise; final score is the mean. With ~2 anchors per draft and 4 metrics, the threshold of 0.5 means "at least half the metrics within 3σ AND at least one within 1σ" (in expectation). This is reasonable but is genuinely a guess pre-data.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Start at 0.5, recalibrate after 7 days** (author's recommendation). Pro: matches "Phase 2 is advisory-only" framing; recalibration is cheap when scores are in the cron output already. Con: 7-day calibration window means the threshold is wrong-but-non-blocking for a week.
  - **(B) Start at a more conservative 0.4** (less likely to log false-warning). Pro: fewer noisy warnings during the calibration window. Con: less informative signal during calibration.
  - **(C) Start at 0.6** (more aggressive). Pro: shorter time to "we should hard-gate" signal. Con: probably triggers warnings on perfectly-fine prose during early runs while the heuristic is being trusted.
- **Prior art:** Slop_penalty's threshold (3.0 prod, 5.0 Phase 1) was also chosen heuristically and revisited after the first batch of real outputs — Phase 1 spec § 5 T6 used 5.0; the Phase 2 spec moves to 3.0 production. Same pattern fits here.
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT** option (A) — start at 0.5. The author's recommended 7-day calibration is the right window. Add one piece of process discipline: the calibration data should land in a `bd-b5p.5.X` study bead (typed `study`) so the recalibration evidence is auditable, not lost to cron logs that rotate.

**Settlement: ACCEPT** with a process discipline addition (calibration data lands in a study bead).

**Spec impact:** §6 OQ-4 — replace "Recalibrate before Phase 3" with "After 7 days of cron data, file a `study`-typed bead with the score distribution; recalibrate from that evidence."

---

## Decision: OQ-5 — DEFER (to Phase 3) — DECIDED 2026-05-27

**Question:** `identity/voice_priors.json` contains structured voice constraints. Should the anchor_selector cross-reference voice_priors to weight anchors?

**Author's Recommendation:** Punt to Phase 3. Phase 2's anchor_selector is POV-only; voice_priors stays as system-prompt content via `build_draft_system`.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** `voice_priors.json` is consumed by `build_draft_system` as flattened JSON in the system-prompt identity block (per Phase 1 runner.py line 189). The anchor_selector (§4.3) only filters on POV match. Cross-referencing voice_priors would let the selector prefer, e.g., anchors that demonstrate the "embodied metaphor as anchor" device when voice_priors says the brief wants that register.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Defer to Phase 3** (author's recommendation). Pro: keeps Phase 2 atomic; voice_priors already influences the system prompt so it isn't being ignored — it just isn't influencing anchor *selection*. Con: leaves a known refinement unexplored.
  - **(B) Add weighting in Phase 2** — score each anchor's "devices demonstrated" against voice_priors' preferred-shapes list, sort by score within POV match. Pro: better anchor quality. Con: scope creep; the entry-parsing in `anchor_selector.py` would need a "devices" extractor too; this is a Phase 3-shaped concern.
  - **(C) Drop voice_priors entirely** in favor of anchor-only voice signal. Pro: simpler. Con: voice_priors carries forbidden-register info that doesn't naturally fall out of anchor stats; would degrade voice fidelity.
- **Prior art:** Two-stage relevance (filter then rank) is a stock IR pattern; doing filter-only in v1 and adding ranker in v2 is the right shape (Phase 2 = filter; Phase 3 = ranker).
- **Recommendation:** **DEFER to Phase 3** — confirms author's rec.

**Settlement: DEFER.** Trigger: Phase 3 spec writing (bd-b5p.6 or successor). Re-open as `OQ-5` in that spec.

**Spec impact:** none in bd-b5p.5. Note in Phase 3 spec when written.

---

## Decision: OQ-6 — DEFER (to Phase 3) — DECIDED 2026-05-27

**Question:** Phase 2 hardcodes the same brief Phase 1 used. When does brief reading from `briefs/<slug>.json` land — Phase 3 or carved out into a Phase 2.5?

**Author's Recommendation:** DECISION VENUE: Phase 3 spec writing. If brief-reading is small enough to slot into Phase 2 without scope creep, fold it; otherwise it's the first Phase 3 deliverable.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** Phase 2's spec §3.6 already lists 5+ brief-derived fields it hardcodes (title, fandom, rating, tags, summary, body). Adding `briefs/*.json` reading is non-trivial: schema definition, validation, slug resolution, fallback semantics. The Phase 2 scope is already three orthogonal axes (delegate_task migration + voice anchors + AO3 staging); adding brief-file reading makes it four.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Defer to Phase 3** (full scope). Pro: Phase 2 stays atomic; brief-reading deserves its own spec since `briefs/<slug>.json` schema is a real artifact, not just a config knob. Con: Phase 2's hardcoded brief duplication with Phase 1 means an A/B comparison on the *same* brief, which is actually a feature (controls for brief-as-variable).
  - **(B) Carve out a Phase 2.5** for brief-reading alone. Pro: ships earlier than waiting for full Phase 3 scope. Con: introduces phase number proliferation; the bd-b5p.X naming is already strained.
  - **(C) Slot brief-reading into Phase 2** as a 4th axis. Pro: ships fully feature-complete sooner. Con: violates the spec's stated three-axis scope; risks Phase 2 slipping.
- **Prior art:** Phase 1 already established the "hardcode the brief, validate the pipeline" pattern. Repeating it in Phase 2 is the principled choice — the brief shouldn't be a variable while validating delegate_task + voice anchors + AO3 staging in parallel.
- **Recommendation:** **DEFER** option (A) — full Phase 3.

**Settlement: DEFER to Phase 3.** Trigger: Phase 3 spec writing, brief-file reading should be its first explicit deliverable.

**Spec impact:** none in bd-b5p.5; the §3.8 non-goal already lists it. When Phase 3 spec lands, mark it as resolving OQ-6.

---

## Decision: OQ-7 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT (with operator-policy clarification)

**Question:** if Phase 2 is rolled back mid-week (T-E-3), what happens to Phase 2's already-enqueued items in `publish_queue/`?

**Author's Recommendation:** they stay. `publish_queue/` is human-review queue; operator inspects entries' author_notes (which include `bd-b5p.5`) and decides per-item to delete, retain for manual posting, or carry forward.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** `publish_queue/*.json` is the existing human-review seam (per spec §2 baseline table — `api/queue.py::enqueue` is unchanged). Items have `status=pending` until promoted. The decision to roll back the Phase 2 cron is operational; the queue items are content.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Keep on rollback, operator decides per-item** (author's recommendation). Pro: respects operator autonomy; the bead-ID tag in `author_notes` makes provenance traceable. Con: requires the operator to know what to do; no automated cleanup.
  - **(B) Auto-purge Phase 2 items on rollback.** Pro: clean state. Con: destroys human content; possibly fine content; violates the "publish_queue is human review" contract.
  - **(C) Auto-mark Phase 2 items as `quarantined` on rollback.** Pro: keeps content but flags it. Con: requires `quarantined` status to exist in `api/queue.py` (it doesn't); scope creep; adds a state machine transition for an event that may never happen.
- **Prior art:** Standard production rollback discipline (e.g., feature-flag rollbacks in webapps) is "queue persists, downstream handles." This matches.
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT** with one operator-policy clarification: document in the §3.7 cron registration block (or in the new Phase 2 SKILL.md body) that "rollback discipline" means "operator runs `grep -l bd-b5p.5 publish_queue/*.json` to enumerate Phase 2 items, then reviews each." A one-line README-style note avoids the next agent guessing.

**Settlement: ACCEPT** with the documented `grep` enumeration recipe added to SKILL.md.

**Spec impact:** §3.7 or §4.1 SKILL.md body — add a "Rollback notes" subsection with the enumeration recipe.

---

## Decision: OQ-8 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT

**Question:** should the parent agent run `evaluate.py` as a sandboxed terminal_tool call or as in-process import?

**Author's Recommendation:** in-process import for Phase 2. The parent agent IS Python; evaluate.py is pure (no side effects). Phase 1's runner.py already does this without issue. Re-evaluate if Hermes plugin isolation becomes a concern.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** Phase 1 runner.py line 295-299 already in-process imports `evaluate.slop_score`. No observed issues across the Phase 1 acceptance run (`slop_penalty=0.0` on Karlach POV sample). `evaluate.py` is read-only on its inputs and returns a dict — textbook pure function.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) In-process import** (author's recommendation). Pro: zero subprocess overhead (~50ms saved); shared sys.path is harmless since evaluate.py has no side effects; Phase 1 validates the pattern. Con: shares Python module cache with Hermes — if a future evaluator imports something Hermes also imports at a different version, there could be a conflict. Unlikely in practice.
  - **(B) terminal_tool subprocess** for clean isolation. Pro: bulletproof isolation; matches Hermes' "tools are out-of-process" mental model. Con: ~50ms overhead per call (negligible at one call per cron firing); requires structured stdout parsing or a tempfile; adds failure modes (subprocess crash, timeout, parsing).
  - **(C) Hermes plugin wrapping evaluate.py** with a `slop_score` tool registered as a Hermes tool. Pro: maximally clean; portable to other skills. Con: substrate fork (registers a custom tool); blocked by the same plugin-authoring scope that OQ-1 contingency-B is blocked by.
- **Prior art:** Anthropic's MCP servers and Claude Code's skills both routinely in-process-import pure functions; subprocess isolation is reserved for code that has side effects or stateful resources.
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT** option (A) — in-process import. Re-evaluation trigger explicit: any Hermes plugin author needing to consume slop_score from a non-Python skill body would force a move to option (C); that's a Phase 3+ concern.

**Settlement: ACCEPT.**

**Spec impact:** none. §4.5 / §3.8 already encode this.

---

## Decision: OQ-9 — DEFER (Phase 3 investigation) — DECIDED 2026-05-27

**Question:** does Hermes' SessionDB capture the delegate child's trajectory? bd-b5p.2 §2 highlights `agent/trajectory.py` ShareGPT export as a Phase 4 voice-training enabler.

**Author's Recommendation:** out of scope for Phase 2 acceptance. Verify empirically during Phase 2 operation and document; if absent, file a Hermes upstream FR or build the hook in Phase 3.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** Phase 2 doesn't need trajectory capture to ship; the deliverable is "queue entry in publish_queue/<id>.json" not "training-data export." But Phase 4 (voice fine-tuning) hinges on trajectory data being available, so this is a real downstream dependency.
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Defer; verify during operation** (author's recommendation). Pro: doesn't block Phase 2; Phase 2 cron operation generates the natural verification surface (just inspect SessionDB after first run). Con: ambiguous owner — who actually does the verification?
  - **(B) Add a Phase 2 acceptance check** that grep's SessionDB for the child's trajectory after T-E-1 runs. Pro: forces resolution. Con: extends Phase 2 acceptance scope; arguably should be a separate `study` bead.
  - **(C) Write the hook now** in Phase 2. Pro: future-proofs Phase 4. Con: major scope creep; the hook design depends on what's actually missing, which we don't yet know.
- **Prior art:** Hermes' SessionDB is documented in primer §3 — sessions persist across process restarts; delegate child runs as a separate sub-session per `_resolve_delegation_credentials`. So the data should be there; the question is whether `agent/trajectory.py` ShareGPT export reaches into sub-sessions or only the parent.
- **Recommendation:** **DEFER** with action: file a `study`-typed sibling bead immediately ("study: verify Hermes SessionDB captures delegate child trajectory for Phase 4 ShareGPT export") so the verification has an owner. The study bead is small (a `br show` of the SessionDB schema + one cron-firing inspection) and doesn't block Phase 2 ship.

**Settlement: DEFER (to a sibling `study` bead).**

**Spec impact:** none in bd-b5p.5 directly; orchestrator should create the study bead before Phase 2 implementation starts so the verification happens in parallel with the implementation wave.

---

## Decision: OQ-10 — DECIDED 2026-05-27 — ACCEPT (with explicit allowlist plan)

**Question:** does the cron prompt-injection scanner flag creative-fic content (BG3 character names, dialogue marks, etc.) as injection candidates?

**Author's Recommendation:** monitor first 7 days of Phase 2 cron firings for `CronPromptInjectionBlocked` events. If triggered, file a Hermes config/allowlist change.

**Expert Analysis:**
- **Current state:** Per primer §5.4 and §6.3, the scanner runs on the **fully-assembled** prompt (cron hint + user prompt + script output + context_from + skill content) for cron jobs specifically. Phase 1 cron has fired without triggering (per Phase 2 spec §6 OQ-10's note "same monitoring posture Phase 1 documented but never triggered"). The Phase 2 brief is fiction with dialogue, character names, and prose-shaped content — none of which historically pattern-matches injection (prompt injection has structural tells: "Ignore previous instructions," role-impersonation, instruction-shape text).
- **Alternatives:**
  - **(A) Monitor first 7 days** (author's recommendation). Pro: low cost; matches Phase 1 posture. Con: if it triggers on day 4, the cron silently fails for days until operator notices.
  - **(B) Pre-allowlist** the Phase 2 skill ahead of first firing. Pro: zero risk of silent failure. Con: requires knowing the right allowlist mechanism in Hermes 0.14 (primer doesn't enumerate one — there's `CronPromptInjectionBlocked` but no documented bypass beyond raising the exception ceiling). Likely needs a Hermes upstream FR for a per-skill allowlist.
  - **(C) Pre-test** by running the assembled Phase 2 prompt through the scanner offline (if Hermes exposes the scanner API standalone). Pro: empirical answer before first cron firing. Con: depends on scanner being accessible from CLI; un-investigated in the primer.
- **Prior art:** Phase 1 monitoring posture didn't trigger; prose content is structurally distinct from injection attempts; the risk is low but real. The unknown unknown is whether qwen3-coder-generated prose ever happens to contain injection-shape text (unlikely with `_PROSE_FRAME`).
- **Recommendation:** **ACCEPT** option (A) — monitor for 7 days. ADD: explicit operator action protocol: "If `CronPromptInjectionBlocked` fires once, manually capture the assembled prompt from cron output for forensics, then file a Hermes upstream issue with the offending content. If it fires twice with different content, suspend the Phase 2 cron pending Hermes allowlist support." This converts the open-ended "file a Hermes change" into a concrete protocol.

**Settlement: ACCEPT** with the two-strikes operator protocol added.

**Spec impact:** §6 OQ-10 body — add the "two strikes" protocol sentence.

---

## Implementation Readiness — 2026-05-27

**P1 OQs decided:** 3/3 (OQ-1, OQ-2, OQ-3 all ACCEPT)
**P2 OQs decided:** 4/4 (OQ-4 ACCEPT-with-process-add; OQ-7 ACCEPT-with-doc; OQ-8 ACCEPT; OQ-10 ACCEPT-with-protocol)
**P3 OQs decided:** 3/3 (OQ-5 DEFER; OQ-6 DEFER; OQ-9 DEFER-to-study-bead)
**Conflicts resolved:** N/A (no cross-spec conflicts surfaced in this walk)
**Dependencies confirmed:** bd-b5p.4 (Phase 1) is CLOSED; bd-b5p.5 has parent-child link to bd-b5p; Hermes Agent 0.14.0 baseline operational per §2.

### Subsystem readiness

- **delegate_task migration (§3.2 + §4.2):** READY. OQ-1 ACCEPT means the design as written stands. T-D-2/T-D-3/T-D-4 are the empirical gates; failure paths are documented under OQ-1 and OQ-3 contingencies.
- **Voice anchor selection (§3.4 + §4.3):** READY. OQ-5 deferred (Phase 3 ranker layer); Phase 2 ships POV-filter-only as specified.
- **Voice-match heuristic (§3.5 + §4.4):** READY. OQ-4 ACCEPT — threshold 0.5, recalibrate after 7 days via study bead.
- **AO3 staging (§3.6 + §4.5):** READY. OQ-7 ACCEPT with operator rollback recipe to be documented in SKILL.md.
- **Cron registration (§3.7):** READY. OQ-10 ACCEPT — first-7-day monitoring with two-strikes protocol.

### Spec updates needed (re-dispatch /spec to apply — non-blocking but recommended)

These are minor amendments; impl can proceed against the existing spec without them, but a spec re-dispatch would clean up the record:

1. **§5 T-D-2 EXPECTED line** — add the `_build_child_system_prompt` boilerplate-dump capture as part of test artifacts.
2. **§3.2 (DECISION on max_iterations block tail)** — add one-sentence summary of the OQ-3 contingency ladder (frame → preface-pre-strengthen → plugin fork).
3. **§3.7 (cron registration) OR §4.1 (SKILL.md body)** — add a "Rollback notes" subsection with the `grep -l bd-b5p.5 publish_queue/*.json` enumeration recipe.
4. **§6 OQ-4 body** — replace "Recalibrate before Phase 3" with "After 7 days of cron data, file a `study`-typed bead with the score distribution; recalibrate from that evidence."
5. **§6 OQ-10 body** — add the "two strikes" operator protocol sentence.

### Recommended impl order

1. **identity_loader.py** (no deps, smallest unit; extracts from Phase 1 runner.py; backfills T-D-4 first).
2. **anchor_selector.py** (depends on identity_loader for few_shot_bank text input; covers T-V-1, T-V-2).
3. **voice_match.py** (independent of selector but needs selector's output to test integration; covers T-V-3, T-V-4).
4. **SKILL.md body + delegate_task invocation** (depends on 1-3; covers T-D-1/2/3, T-A-1/2/3/4/5).
5. **staging-test.sh** (integration harness; covers T-E-1).
6. **Cron registration + Phase 1 coexistence** (T-E-2, T-E-3).

### Parallel work (orchestrator should dispatch alongside Phase 2 impl)

- **study bead: SessionDB trajectory capture for delegate children** (OQ-9 resolution) — independent investigation; can run in parallel.
- **study bead: Hermes upstream `skill_view(raw=True)` feature request** (OQ-2 carry-forward) — independent; can wait until Phase 2 ships.

### No blockers surfaced.

All P1 OQs are decidable from existing context (Hermes primer + Phase 1 evidence + delegate_tool.py code). The empirical-gate OQs (OQ-1/2/3 via T-D-2/3/4) are resolvable during the implementation wave itself, not pre-implementation. Phase 2 spec is **READY for /test → /impl dispatch.**

---
